title: Runner As Core User-Plane
description:
Goal: make octo-runner the mandatory user-plane boundary. The octo backend becomes primarily auth/routing/proxy with a minimal control-plane DB. All user data + user operations (sessions, filesystem ops, mmry lifecycle/ports, main-chat state) live behind per-user runners, enabling OS-level isolation locally and clean extension to remote runner nodes later.

## Non-Goals (initial phase)
- Remote runner nodes over network transport (design for it, but implement local Unix socket first)
- Replacing container isolation model on macOS/Windows (container mode remains primary there)

## Requirements
- Physically impossible cross-user access in local linux multi-user mode (no backend direct filesystem/DB reads of other users)
- Deterministic per-user state location (db + ports + runner socket)
- Streaming support for opencode/pi outputs (no loss of current streaming UX)
- Backwards compatible migration path with feature flags

## Deliverables
- Runner RPC surface expanded to cover user-plane operations
- Backend refactored to call runner for user-plane operations
- Control-plane DB reduced to non-user data only
- Setup tooling + systemd units updated
- Tests + security checks

## Acceptance Criteria
- In local linux multi-user mode, backend cannot read other users' workspaces or per-user DB paths (even if compromised), because it only connects to user-specific runner sockets.
- All endpoints (sessions list, file viewer, terminal, memories, main chat) source data from the authenticated user's userspace.
- Creating a new user provisions linux user, runner socket dir, and per-user db/state without requiring interactive sudo.
- Existing single-user local mode continues to work.
- Container mode continues to work without requiring runner.

title: Runner API: user-plane session registry
description:
Add runner RPC endpoints for per-user session state:
- create/get/list/stop/resume/delete sessions (local runtime)
- persist session metadata in per-user store (DB or JSON) owned by the linux user
- include workspace path validation against runner-owned workspace roots
- return ports + status + errors

Notes:
- Backend must never read per-user session DB directly in multi-user mode.
- API should be designed so it can later be exposed over remote transport (mTLS WS).

title: Test plan: isolation matrix
description:
Add automated tests / manual checklist for:
- local single-user
- local linux multi-user (2 users) verifying no cross access to sessions/files/memories/main chat
- container multi-user

Include: attempt to access other user's session_id, workspace_path, mmry store; expect 404/permission error.

title: Runner API: per-user file operations
description:
Define + implement runner-side filesystem API used by the backend:
- list tree, read file, write file, mkdir, stat
- path validation against per-user workspace roots (no traversal)
- optional read/write quotas

Goal: backend file endpoints proxy to runner, not to backend-owned paths.

title: Runner API: per-user mmry lifecycle
description:
Move mmry lifecycle/port ownership fully into runner:
- allocate stable per-user mmry port (persisted in per-user db)
- spawn/stop/pin mmry process for that linux user
- health check + log capture
- expose mmry external_api URL for backend proxy

Goal: backend mmry proxy calls runner to resolve target; backend does not spawn mmry.

title: Runner API: main chat storage ownership
description:
Make main chat data physically per-user:
- move main chat DB + session files into linux user's home dir
- runner provides APIs for main chat list/create/load/save
- migrate existing shared main chat data into per-user location

Goal: user cannot see another user's main chat even if backend is compromised.

title: Backend: route all user operations through runner
description:
Refactor backend handlers/proxies so all user-plane operations are served via runner:
- sessions list/create/resume/stop
- terminal + opencode process lifecycle
- workspace file viewer
- memories (mmry target resolution)
- main chat persistence

Backend retains auth and request routing only.

title: Security: runner socket authz + strict path guards
description:
Harden the runner boundary:
- unix socket permissions via /run/octo/runner-sockets/<linux_username>/octo-runner.sock (group octo)
- runner validates every request against its own user roots
- deny dangerous env vars, validate binary allowlist
- ensure backend cannot ask runner to read outside workspace/main-chat dirs

Add negative tests for traversal and cross-user access.

title: Local linux provisioning: non-interactive sudo + tmpfiles
description:
Make user provisioning deterministic and non-interactive:
- ensure /run/octo/runner-sockets exists via tmpfiles
- ensure per-user runner directories (2770, setgid) are created at user creation
- ensure linger + octo-runner user unit enabled
- verify runner socket reachable after provisioning

Goal: no sudo prompts during requests.

title: Compatibility: container mode runner adapter
description:
Keep docker multi-user working:
- define how runner API maps to container runtime
- either run runner inside container, or implement a backend adapter that satisfies runner interface using container APIs
- ensure streaming still works

Goal: macOS docker deployments remain supported.

title: Observability: runner logs + health endpoints
description:
Add runner health + diagnostics:
- ping/status endpoint
- per-process status + stdout/stderr tail
- structured events for start/stop/crash
- backend exposes aggregated diagnostics for admins

title: Android Emulator: Agent-controlled Android environment
Android: Emulator lifecycle management (AVD/Cuttlefish per session)
description:
Manage Android emulator lifecycle per session.

## Options
1. **AVD (Android Virtual Device)** - Standard emulator
   - emulator -avd <name> -no-window -gpu swiftshader_indirect
   - Pros: Full Android, Play Store support
   - Cons: Heavy (~2-4GB RAM per instance)

2. **Cuttlefish** - Google's cloud-optimized emulator
   - Designed for headless/server use
   - Better resource efficiency
   - Used by AndroidWorld benchmark

3. **Genymotion** - Commercial alternative
   - Good performance
   - Licensing required

## Implementation
- Start emulator on session create (if Android enabled)
- ADB connect to emulator
- Stop emulator on session end
- Resource limits per user
- Snapshot/restore for fast startup

## Reference
- ../external-repos/android_world - Uses Cuttlefish with gRPC
- AndroidLab - Uses preloaded AVD snapshots
close_reason: Implemented emulator.py with EmulatorManager: AVD creation, session management with persistent userdata, snapshots, headless mode

```
agent-android/
```

title: Android: UI tree extraction via accessibility service
description:
Extract and parse Android UI tree for element references.

## Data Source
```bash
adb shell uiautomator dump /dev/stdout
```

Returns XML with elements containing:
- resource-id, class, text, content-desc
- bounds (clickable area)
- clickable, focusable, scrollable flags

## Output Format (like agent-browser refs)
```json
{
  "elements": [
    {
      "ref": "@e1",
      "class": "android.widget.Button",
      "text": "Sign In",
      "resource_id": "com.app:id/login_btn",
      "bounds": { "x": 100, "y": 500, "w": 200, "h": 50 },
      "clickable": true
    }
  ],
  "tree": "<compact text representation>"
}
```

## Ref Assignment
- Assign stable refs (@e1, @e2, ...) to interactive elements
- Prioritize by: clickable > focusable > has-text
- Include hierarchy context for disambiguation

## Reference
- ../external-repos/android_world - accessibility forwarding
- AndroidLab XML mode
close_reason: Implemented in snapshot.py with full accessibility fields

title: Opencode agent connection cycling - rapid disconnect/reconnect loop
description:
The opencode agent repeatedly disconnects and reconnects in rapid succession (observed 15+ cycles in logs). Pattern observed:

[Event] agent_disconnected
[Event] agent_reconnecting  
[Event] agent_connected
[Event] opencode_event (x4)

This cycle repeats continuously, causing downstream 503 errors on all /code/ endpoint requests. The root cause is likely:
1. Opencode process crashing (OOM or unhandled error)
2. WebSocket connection instability
3. Bug in reconnection logic causing retry storm

Impact: Users cannot interact with opencode sessions during disconnection windows. All proxied requests fail with 503.
close_reason: Opencode is deprecated

title: Tabbed center pane: chat + multi-file tabs with per-chat state and workspace pins
description:
Implement multi-file tabs in the central chat window.

Requested behavior:
- Central pane supports multiple tabs where chat itself is one tab.
- Users can open multiple files; each open file appears as its own tab beside chat.
- Mobile behavior: only one file tab visible at a time, with a switcher to change among open files.
- Open files are persisted per chat/session, so switching chats restores each chat's own open-file tab set.
- File tab hover action: "Pin file". Pinned files open as tabs for every chat in the same workdir.
- In shared workspaces, add "Pin for everyone" so selected files open as tabs for all users in that workspace.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Desktop: chat + N file tabs render in central pane and can be switched/closed.
2) Mobile: single active file tab + file switcher UI; no multi-tab overflow requirement.
3) Per-chat persistence works across chat switching and reload.
4) Workdir-level pin applies across chats for that user.
5) Shared workspace "pin for everyone" propagates and persists for collaborators.
6) Clear distinction between user-local pins and workspace-global pins in data model/API.
7) Include migration/compat plan for existing chat state (no legacy fallback required, but no data loss).

title: oqto-serve: agent web application server
description:
Implement oqto-serve, a CLI + HTTP server that lets agents serve self-contained HTML/CSS/JS apps to the user within the oqto frontend.

NOTE: Single-file HTML apps are handled by oqto-yxhc (inline HTML apps via srcdoc iframes, no server needed). oqto-serve is for multi-file apps with relative imports, TypeScript transpilation, and CDN passthrough.

Architecture:
- oqto-serve CLI (Rust binary): start/stop/list/scaffold commands
- Static file serving with swc TypeScript transpilation (no Node/Bun)
- Backend: port allocation (50000-50999), reverse proxy with auth, WsEvents
- Frontend: ServeView iframe panel with hot reload
- File watcher (notify crate) triggers reload on changes

Apps use the byteowlz app runtime contract (--app-* CSS vars, window.apphost bridge) from oqto-dbbw. oqto-serve injects the host mapping layer (hardcoded oqto palette initially, dynamic when theming lands).

Templates ship embedded in the binary. Agent can scaffold from templates or create from scratch.

Full design: docs/design/oqto-serve.md

Depends on: oqto-dbbw (app runtime contract), oqto-yxhc (shared apphost bridge)
Supersedes: octo-58xa (Agent WebView -- proxy approach absorbed here)

title: oqto-serve CLI: static file server with swc TypeScript transpilation
description:
Implement the oqto-serve Rust binary with start/stop/list subcommands.

start flow:
1. Contact oqto backend to allocate port (POST /serve/start)
2. Bind HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:{port}
3. Serve static files with correct MIME types
4. .ts files transpiled to JS on-the-fly via swc crate (cached in memory)
5. CDN imports (esm.sh, cdn.jsdelivr.net) pass through untouched
6. Inject --app-* CSS mapping and apphost shim into served index.html
7. Start file watcher (notify crate) on workdir
8. On file change: invalidate transpile cache, POST /serve/reload
9. Stay running until killed or oqto-serve stop

New crate: backend/crates/oqto-serve/

title: Backend: serve API routes and port allocation
description:
Add serve management routes to the oqto backend:

Routes:
- POST /serve/start -- allocate port, register instance, broadcast WsEvent::ServeStart
- POST /serve/reload -- broadcast WsEvent::ServeReload
- POST /serve/stop -- deregister, free port, broadcast WsEvent::ServeStop
- GET /serve/list -- list active instances for current user
- GET /serve/{id}/* -- reverse proxy to localhost:{port} with auth check
- POST /serve/heartbeat -- CLI pings every 30s for liveness

Config section:
[serve]
enabled = true
port_range_start = 50000
port_range_end = 50999
max_per_session = 5
heartbeat_timeout_seconds = 90

DB table: serve_instances (id, session_id, user_id, port, workdir, title, status, last_heartbeat, created_at)

New WsEvent variants: ServeStart, ServeReload, ServeStop

Lifecycle cleanup: prune on session end, heartbeat timeout, backend restart.

title: Frontend: ServeView iframe panel with hot reload
description:
New view in the session screen alongside chat, files, terminal, browser.

- New ViewKey: "serve" added to the type union
- WsEvent::ServeStart -> store instance metadata, auto-switch to serve view
- WsEvent::ServeReload -> increment iframe key (triggers full page reload)
- WsEvent::ServeStop -> remove instance, switch to previous view
- Multiple instances: tabbed UI in serve panel
- iframe sandbox: allow-scripts allow-forms allow-same-origin
- Theme forwarding: postMessage({type: "apphost:theme", theme: "dark"|"light"}) on theme change and initial load
- oqtoctl integration: oqtoctl ui panel --view serve

title: oqto-serve scaffold command with embedded templates
description:
Implement the scaffold subcommand with embedded templates (include_dir! or equivalent).

Templates (all using --app-* tokens from byteowlz app runtime contract):
- blank: minimal index.html + style.css + empty main.ts
- dashboard: grid of stat cards, data table, status indicators
- diagram: Mermaid diagram with zoom/pan, dark/light theme config
- chart: responsive Chart.js chart with data loading
- table: sortable, filterable data table with pagination
- form: multi-section form with validation
- deck: multi-slide presentation with navigation
- recap: project recap page with sections, KPI cards, status grid

Each template is a complete working example using --app-* vars and window.apphost for theming. Templates include the apphost-shim.js and app-tokens.css.

Usage: oqto-serve scaffold dashboard --output ./metrics

title: ServeView: postMessage bridge for apphost.send/onMessage
description:
Implement the bidirectional messaging channel between served apps and the agent session.

Outbound (app -> agent):
- iframe calls apphost.send(data)
- apphost shim does window.parent.postMessage({type: "apphost:send", data}, "*")
- ServeView component receives postMessage, validates origin matches serve proxy
- Forwards data to agent session via WebSocket mux as a new command type (e.g. cmd: "app_message")
- Runner delivers to Pi stdin as a structured message

Inbound (agent -> app):
- Agent calls a tool or emits a message targeting the served app (e.g. oqto-serve notify)
- Runner broadcasts as a new WsEvent (e.g. ServeMessage { serve_id, data })
- ServeView receives event, does iframe.contentWindow.postMessage({type: "apphost:message", data}, "*")
- apphost shim receives it, calls registered onMessage callbacks

Security:
- Validate postMessage origins
- Rate limit outbound messages from app
- Max message size

Depends on: oqto-14b1.3 (ServeView), oqto-dbbw.3 (apphost shim)

title: Runner: app_message command and ServeMessage event routing
description:
Add support in the runner for bidirectional app messaging.

Inbound (frontend -> agent):
- New mux command: {channel: "agent", cmd: "app_message", session_id, serve_id, data}
- Runner receives, formats as structured input for Pi (e.g. JSON on stdin or a special tool response)
- Pi agent sees the message as user input from the served app

Outbound (agent -> frontend):
- Agent calls a tool like oqto-serve send or writes to a known channel
- Runner translates to canonical event: ServeMessage { serve_id, data }
- Backend broadcasts to frontend via WebSocket

Alternative: agent uses oqto-serve CLI to send messages (oqto-serve send --data {...}), which POSTs to backend, which broadcasts.

Depends on: oqto-14b1.2 (backend serve routes)

title: Serve proxy: apphost.fetch, readFile, writeFile capabilities
description:
Implement optional capabilities on the apphost bridge in oqto.

apphost.fetch(url, opts):
- App posts fetch request via postMessage to ServeView
- ServeView proxies through backend (bypasses iframe CORS restrictions)
- Response streamed back via postMessage
- Rate limited, allowlist/denylist configurable

apphost.readFile(path) / writeFile(path, data):
- Workspace-scoped file access
- Routed through oqto-files (existing file server)
- Path validation: must be within session workspace, no traversal
- readFile returns ArrayBuffer, writeFile accepts ArrayBuffer or string

apphost.saveState(key, value) / loadState(key):
- Per-app persistent state (survives page reload, session restart)
- Stored in serve_instances metadata or a dedicated KV table
- Key scoped to serve_id

apphost.clipboard.read() / .write(text):
- Proxied through ServeView which uses navigator.clipboard (has permission as main frame)

All capabilities are optional -- apps feature-detect with apphost.fetch?.() pattern.

Depends on: oqto-14b1.5 (postMessage bridge)

title: Session duplication in frontend sidebar - platform_id not set in hstry
description: Sessions created from the oqto frontend appear duplicated in the sidebar. Root cause: when a Pi session is created via the runner, the hstry conversation entry gets external_id (Pi native ID) but platform_id (oqto session ID) is NULL. The merge_duplicate_sessions() dedup logic in chat.rs can't match the hstry entry with the runner's live session because they have different IDs. Example: oqto session oqto-2a079205 spawned Pi session 584ac6ee which hstry stores with readable_id=used-fort-save, but no platform_id linking back to the oqto ID.

title: Fix fragile string-based error classification in runner client
Critical: tokio::broadcast channel overflow silently drops streaming events
description:
The runner uses tokio::broadcast::channel(256) for distributing Pi events to WebSocket subscribers. When Pi emits events faster than the WebSocket can consume them (rapid text_delta bursts during long tool outputs), broadcast::Receiver::recv() returns Lagged(n), permanently dropping n events.

This causes: missing text deltas, missing tool results, missing agent_end/agent_idle events. The frontend may never learn a turn completed.

Current mitigation (stream.resync_required) is reactive -- the damage is already done.

Proposed fix: Replace tokio::broadcast with per-subscriber mpsc::UnboundedSender channels. Each WebSocket subscription gets its own unbounded channel. Events are cloned into each subscriber. No backpressure, no drops. Memory cost is negligible at our scale (1-10 concurrent connections per user).

Files:
- backend/crates/oqto/src/runner/pi_manager.rs: event_tx broadcast channel
- backend/crates/oqto/src/pi/runtime.rs: event_tx in LocalPiProcess, RunnerPiProcess
- backend/crates/oqto/src/api/ws_multiplexed.rs: forward_pi_events subscription

See: docs/design/message-sync-invariants.md for full analysis.

title: Stability: hstry gRPC high-availability with local spool fallback
description:
hstry is currently a single point of failure. If the hstry gRPC service becomes unavailable, all message persistence fails with no graceful degradation. This is especially critical for long-running agent sessions where losing message history is unacceptable.

## Problem Statement

From AGENTS.md: "All chat history access goes through hstry's gRPC API - no raw SQLite access from oqto." This creates a hard dependency on hstry availability.

## Requirements

1. Local Write-Ahead Spooling
   - Runner maintains local SQLite spool for pending writes
   - Async forward to hstry with exponential backoff retry
   - Automatic replay on hstry reconnection
   - Deduplication via message ID to prevent double-writes

2. Read Resilience
   - Local LRU cache for recent message queries
   - Stale cache serving with warning flag when hstry unavailable
   - Frontend visual indicator for "history sync pending"

3. Health Monitoring
   - hstry health check endpoint
   - Circuit breaker pattern for gRPC calls
   - Alerting on spool depth threshold

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Messages written to local spool immediately, forwarded async
- [ ] hstry downtime does not block session operations
- [ ] Messages replay correctly after hstry recovery
- [ ] No message loss on runner crash (fsync strategy)
- [ ] Frontend shows sync status indicator

## Related
- oqto-runner already reads hstry SQLite directly (per AGENTS.md)
- This extends that pattern for resilience, not just performance

title: oqto-sandbox: landlock audit mode silently enforces, breaking Claude Code and Codex harnesses
description: Repro: oqto-sandbox claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions hangs silently; pi works. Root cause: apply_landlock in config.rs calls landlock_restrict_self unconditionally when mode != Off. Audit mode is a misnomer — kernel has no audit-only Landlock; once restrict_self runs, rules are enforced. Because the development profile's allow_write omits ~/.claude, ~/.local/share/claude, ~/.codex, ~/.local/share/codex, and related paths, startup writes (SQLite WAL, statsig, store.db, session files) get EACCES and the JS event loop stalls. Fix: skip restrict_self in audit mode (log intended rules only); add Claude Code + Codex paths to the development profile allow_write; verify with tmux pty run.
close_reason: Fixed: landlock Audit mode short-circuits before restrict_self (oqto-sandbox/src/config.rs); regression test test_landlock_audit_does_not_restrict at config.rs:2766. Verified.

title: Scalability: PostgreSQL backend for hstry beyond SQLite limits
description:
hstry uses SQLite which has fundamental scaling limits. Current design supports tens to low-hundreds of users. Enterprise deployments require 1000+ concurrent users with high message throughput.

## SQLite Limitations

- Write contention: ~1-5K writes/sec max
- Single-node only (no replication)
- Connection pool exhaustion under load
- Backup/restore complexity at scale

## Requirements

1. PostgreSQL Backend Option
   - Feature-flagged storage backend
   - Migration path from SQLite (export/import)
   - Connection pooling (deadpool/sqlx)

2. Schema Optimizations
   - Partitioning by date for messages table
   - Proper indexes for common query patterns
   - Read replica support for query offloading

3. Deployment Configurations
   - Single-node: SQLite (default, simple deployments)
   - Multi-node: PostgreSQL (enterprise, HA setups)
   - Managed: Cloud SQL / RDS compatibility

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] hstry compiles with --features postgres
- [ ] PostgreSQL schema matches SQLite semantics
- [ ] Migration tool sqlite-to-postgres
- [ ] Performance: 10K+ writes/sec sustained
- [ ] Horizontal read scaling with replicas

## Related
- hstry gRPC service architecture
- Per-workspace hstry stores (octo-xxe2)

title: Canonical message identity pipeline (no-heuristic chat sync)
description:
Replace the multi-source message reconciliation with a single-authority model: the runner owns an in-memory message buffer that is authoritative for active sessions, hstry is authoritative for inactive sessions, and the frontend never merges from multiple sources.

## Status

Scopes 1-6 complete. Scopes 7-9 (frontend simplification) remain.

### Done
- Scope 1-3 (commit ae1b5fa): Frontend canonical IDs, id/client_id upsert store, runner Pi+hstry tail merge
- Scope 4-5 (commit 489ef06, bf4527b): Runner message buffer as single authority for active sessions. Buffer seeded from hstry, updated on AgentEnd + incremental persist, read directly by get_workspace_chat_session_messages and pi_get_messages. Pi get_messages RPC removed from display path.
- Scope 6 (commit 78c9116): Buffer flushed to hstry on session close for crash recovery.

### Architecture
Active session: Pi stdout -> PiTranslator -> runner message buffer (Vec<ChatMessageProto>)
  - broadcast to frontend (streaming deltas)
  - async persist to hstry (every ~1s + on idle + on close)
  - get_messages returns buffer directly (no Pi RPC)

Inactive session: get_messages -> hstry

### Remaining
- Scope 7: Simplify chat state machine (collapse hstryId/piId -> sessionId, remove MessageSyncSource/MessageMergeMode)
- Scope 8: Frontend simplification (remove merge mode distinction, version-gated fetch, deferred queues)
- Scope 9: Regression test suite (buffer tests, broadcast overflow, disappearing message repro)
close_reason: All 9 scopes complete. Runner message buffer is single authority for active sessions. Frontend state machine simplified. 122 tests pass.

title: Backend architecture refactor: runner-first crate split and module cleanup
description:
Refactor backend toward a runner-first architecture with clear crate boundaries and removal of duplicate/ambiguous module ownership.

Target tree and architecture map:
- docs/architecture/backend-target-tree.md

Scope:
- Make runner-mediated user-plane the default in all normal modes
- Split oversized files (ws_multiplexed, oqto-runner daemon) into coherent submodules
- Extract oqto-runner and oqto-sandbox into dedicated crates
- Consolidate history/hstry responsibilities
- Enforce single canonical protocol type source in oqto-protocol

Constraints:
- Incremental migration with compatibility at each step
- No protocol regressions

Acceptance criteria:
- Fresh dev startup works without manual runner launch.
- If runner dies, backend recovers automatically (or fails with clear actionable message).
- Connection attempts are bounded and logged with reason codes.
- Integration tests simulate unavailable runner and recovery path.

title: Rewrite backend AGENTS.md as Oqto-specific guidance
close_reason: Rewrote backend/AGENTS.md from stale Rust CLI template to a 14-line Oqto backend guide covering crate boundaries, runner/history seams, protocol/generated types, session ID discipline, sandbox/config truth, provisioning preservation, Rust gates, and Claude cargo sandbox caveat. Validation: git diff --check -- backend/AGENTS.md.

title: Fix file tree not showing uploaded files when currently inside target subdirectory
close_reason: Refresh current directory on upload completion and auto-clear completed upload jobs. Fixes subdirectory view showing stale tree after upload and keeps upload row from lingering after successful write.

title: Highlight recently created/modified files in file tree sidebar
close_reason: Added fade highlight to FileTreeView rows: green tint for created files/dirs, amber for modified, ~4s lifetime with single sweep timer; existing transition-colors gives the fade-out. Threaded HighlightMap through TreeView -> TreeRow. Deletes intentionally not highlighted (row disappears on refresh). lint + build clean.

title: Sandbox policy integration for secret isolation
Runner egress policy hardening: add enforceable host-allowlist boundary with audit semantics
description: Implement runner-native egress policy boundary for secret-bearing operations: host/domain allowlist enforcement, deny-by-default behavior, and structured allow/deny reason codes. Use iron-proxy as design reference only; do not introduce external MITM dependency.

title: Backend crate architecture refactor program
description:
Goal: Refactor backend/crates into clear, enforceable architectural boundaries so new contributors can locate ownership quickly and edits compile faster with less cross-crate invalidation.

Strategic agent-runtime decision:
- Pi is Oqto's native first-class harness integration.
- Other harnesses should integrate through a generic ACP bridge by default, not native per-harness crates.
- Native non-Pi integrations require a documented exception with clear ACP insufficiency.
- ACP is also a sub-agent runtime: Pi sessions should be able to create/control ACP sub-agents through runner-owned lifecycle APIs.
- Use ../external-repos/acpx as inspiration for JSON-RPC stdio transport, session lifecycle, queueing/cancel behavior, permission policy, adapter launch ownership, and conformance profiles.

Success criteria:
- `backend/crates/README.md` is authoritative and matches real crate boundaries.
- `crates/oqto` becomes thin composition/wiring; domain logic is extracted into focused crates.
- Runner-owned harness/runtime logic is not embedded in the server crate.
- Dependency direction is enforced in practice (no upward deps into `oqto`).
- Each extraction slice is behavior-preserving first, cleanup second.
- Lint/test/check remain green after every slice.

Program phases:
1) Foundation and guardrails (docs + dependency policy + metrics baseline)
2) Runner/harness boundary cleanup (oqto-pi, runner daemon ownership, ACP architecture)
3) Adapter/service boundary extractions (provisioning and runner-facing APIs)
4) Core domain extractions (users/workspaces/auth/sessions/history)
5) API surface slimming (`oqto-api` transport-only)
6) Compatibility shim removal and final hardening

Primary risks:
- Silent behavior drift during large moves
- Circular dependencies while splitting runner/session/history paths
- Building an adapter zoo instead of using ACP generically
- Overly broad first cuts that stall review

Mitigation:
- Small, reviewable child issues with explicit boundaries
- Compatibility re-exports only as temporary bridges
- Per-slice compile/lint validation and smoke checks
- ACP-first policy for non-Pi harnesses

title: Define and enforce backend crate dependency policy
description: Document enforceable crate dependency direction for backend. Define allowed layers (binaries -> api/wiring -> domain -> adapters -> protocol/core), forbidden edges (library crates depending on oqto server crate), and review checklist. Deliverables: updated backend/crates/README.md policy section and a short PR checklist snippet. Exit criteria: policy merged and referenced by at least one active extraction task.
close_reason: Added backend crate boundary lint to just lint, documented dependency policy in backend/crates/README.md, and allowlisted the single legacy oqto-runner -> oqto edge pending oqto-3ct7.3. Full just lint passes.

title: Split transport: oqto-api (REST) + oqto-gateway (multiplexed WS relay)
description: Per ADR-0008/Q11. Two crates, not one: oqto-api = thin REST adapters over transport-agnostic domain crates; oqto-gateway = the multiplexed-WS relay owning connection/routing state and enforcing route-not-supervise (ADR-0001). Delete proxy/ (container-service + mmry proxy, both dying). oqto-bus lives in oqto-gateway (ADR-0012).

title: Slim oqto crate to composition root only
description: Slim oqto crate to composition root only. Scope: startup, config loading, dependency graph wiring, route mounting, and binary entrypoints. Remove temporary compatibility re-exports once direct crate interfaces are adopted. Deliverables: reduced oqto src structure and updated crate docs. Exit criteria: oqto is thin and architectural ownership is obvious from crate tree.

title: Final hardening: remove legacy shims and verify architecture docs
description: Final hardening pass for the refactor program. Remove remaining compatibility shims, verify crate READMEs match actual ownership, run full lint/test/check gates, and resolve residual warnings. Deliverables: clean dependency graph, accurate docs, and green CI-equivalent local checks. Exit criteria: epic closure with no known structural TODOs in backend/crates.

title: Define Oqto ACP integration architecture
description: Define Oqto's agent integration strategy: Pi is the native first-class integration, while non-Pi harnesses go through an ACP bridge unless there is a documented exception. Use ../external-repos/acpx as inspiration for JSON-RPC stdio transport, session lifecycle, queueing/cancel semantics, permission policy, built-in adapter launch ownership, and conformance profile thinking. Include sub-agent use case: Pi sessions can create/control ACP sub-agents through runner-owned lifecycle APIs rather than shelling out directly.

title: Create oqto-acp crate for generic ACP runtime bridge
description: Implement a generic ACP bridge crate after the architecture is defined. It should own ACP JSON-RPC stdio transport, initialize/session-new/session-prompt/session-cancel, session/update streaming, permission callbacks, adapter process launch, and clear startup errors. This crate is used by oqto-runner both for top-level ACP-compatible agents and Pi-created sub-agents. ACPX is reference material, not necessarily a dependency.

title: Subtractive pass: delete container + local modules, collapse session runtime-mode branching
description: Per ADR-0001/0008. The FIRST decomposition step is subtractive. Delete container/ (1.6K) and local/ (0.8K) modules; collapse session/'s match runtime_mode branching; delete user_plane/direct.rs + the UserPlane trait; delete canon/from_hstry.rs. Execution always goes through a runner, including single-user. Gated only on the runner-only personal path (ADR-0002).

title: Define PlacementStore contract trait (unblocks oqto-sessions extraction)
description: Per ADR-0011. resolve(session_id)->Placement + list_runners(filter). Allocation policy in oqto, not gvnr (ADR-0004 pure registry). Speaks AGENT_CTX ids. oqto-sessions depends on this trait, not on gvnr; a thin local-backed impl unblocks extraction before gvnr-core exists. gvnr is never a prerequisite.

title: Rename oqto-usermgr -> oqto-hostd; broaden to privileged host-mutation broker
description: Per ADR-0009. oqto-host stays the host-primitives lib (linux_users/sandbox/process; runtime.rs deleted); oqto-hostd is the privileged daemon wielding it. Broaden beyond user creation to THE privileged host-mutation broker: socket/unit setup (ADR-0002), sudoers remediation (oqto-vemr.6), privileged runner restart (oqto-m94p) fold in. Layering: oqto-provisioning (plan) -> oqto-hostd (execute) -> oqto-host (primitives).

title: Make terminal a canonical channel through the gateway; retire ttyd HTTP proxy
description: Per Q11/ADR-0008. terminal currently rides an HTTP proxy to container ttyd while files rides the canonical channel. Promote terminal to a canonical channel through oqto-gateway/runner, retiring the ttyd proxy leg of proxy/. Finishes the multiplexed-WS design.

title: Add baseline compile-time and rebuild-scope metrics for refactor tracking
description: Create repeatable baseline metrics before major extractions. Track: cargo check -p oqto, cargo clippy -p oqto, just deploy --dry-run local build stage timing, and one representative incremental rebuild after editing host/session files. Deliverables: command list, machine assumptions, and baseline table committed to repo docs. Exit criteria: baseline available for before/after comparison in later tasks.
close_reason: Added backend/BUILD-METRICS.md with repeatable timing commands, environment metadata, warm/incremental baselines, and prior cold deploy-fast context. Linked from backend/README.md. Full just lint passes.

title: Extract backend runner socket client paths into oqto-runner-client crate
description: Deleted legacy runner daemon/runtime copies from oqto. oqto now depends on oqto-runner for runner protocol types; oqto/src/runner only contains client/router. Removed oqto/src/runner/{daemon,pi_manager.rs,pi_translator.rs,protocol.rs}. Validation passed: cargo test -p oqto-runner, cargo check -p oqto, just lint.
close_reason: Runner runtime and socket client are now out of oqto. oqto-runner owns protocol, translator, pi_manager, daemon server/handlers, oqto_log projector copy, and RunnerClient. oqto/src/runner now only contains backend-specific target routing over AppState/shared workspaces. Boundary lint is clean and oqto-runner does not depend on oqto.

Move Pi-owned types and session helper modules currently under oqto/src/pi into the oqto-runner library API, then update oqto call sites to use oqto_runner::pi. Kept oqto copies temporarily because oqto still owns client/server/pi_manager and cannot depend on oqto-runner while oqto-runner still depends on oqto. This gives oqto-runner ownership/tests for protocol + translator now; next slice is moving pi_manager/server or extracting protocol to a neutral crate if we want to avoid duplicate transition. close_reason: Moved runner protocol, Pi translator, oqto-log projector, Pi manager, and daemon server/handlers into oqto-runner. oqto-runner binary now uses its own Runner/PiSessionManager and no longer depends on oqto. Removed backend crate boundary allowlist. Validation passed: cargo test -p oqto-runner (47 tests), just lint.

title: Remove oqto-runner dependency on oqto and boundary allowlist

title: Create oqto-provisioning crate and move user setup/sync workflows

Include migration plan for local user_mmry and user_sldr managers where boundaries permit. Deliverables: provisioning service interfaces + moved implementations. title: Extract platform Account domain into oqto-accounts crate (renamed from oqto-users)

Deliverables: oqto-auth crate with clear API for handlers/services. Exit criteria: oqto crate no longer contains core auth business logic except wiring and configuration.

title: Extract session orchestration into oqto-sessions crate

Execute as incremental slices if needed: models first, services next, adapters last. Deliverables: oqto-sessions crate + migrated usage in api and runner-facing paths. Exit criteria: session logic mostly removed from oqto and checks stay green.

title: Extract history and oqto-log access into oqto-history crate

This reduced oqto from 54,840 to 54,396 Rust LOC. Attempted moving projector into oqto-history first, but that would require oqto-history -> oqto-runner for ChatMessageProto and create a dependency cycle because oqto-runner depends on oqto-history; defer until ChatMessageProto is moved to a neutral protocol/history type. Validation passed: cargo test -p oqto-runner, just lint.

title: Extract runner-facing history boundary into oqto-history
description: Runner-facing projector remains in oqto-runner for now because its return type is oqto_runner::protocol::ChatMessageProto. oqto duplicate was deleted/replaced by shim. Next proper cleanup is move ChatMessageProto/part conversion to neutral oqto-history or oqto-protocol, then projector can live in oqto-history without cycles.
close_reason: Runner now calls oqto-history oqto-log projector APIs directly and only converts neutral projected chat DTOs at the runner protocol boundary; removed runner-owned oqto_log_projector shim.

title: Secure secret submission: client -> runner -> SecretProvider (no agent transcript exposure)
description: Per ADR-0007. Path is client -> runner -> SecretProvider, where SecretProvider is pluggable (kyz/Infisical/Vault/env), not kyz-hardcoded. Secrets never enter the agent transcript or env in cleartext.

title: Defer Mermaid diagram rendering until assistant streaming completes
close_reason: Deferred Mermaid rendering in streaming assistant chat groups by adding enableMermaid flag to shared MarkdownRenderer and disabling until turn finalization; lint/build pass.

title: Cross-platform tool portability: XDG-everywhere config dirs + Windows compile
description:
Make byteowlz tools truly cross-platform (audit 2026-06-28). Cross-repo; tracked from the oqto tracker.

KEY FINDING: the canonical config-dir helper ALREADY EXISTS (AppPaths in templates/rust-workspace/crates/rust-core/src/paths.rs, vendored into ~20+ repos). So the work is FIX + RE-PROPAGATE, not write-new.

CONFIG-DIR RULE (option B, decided): an explicit XDG_* env var wins on EVERY OS; otherwise ~/.config-style on all unix INCLUDING macOS (deliberately NOT ~/Library/Application Support for CLI tools); native APPDATA/LOCALAPPDATA on Windows. Current helper is option A (dirs::* fallback -> ~/Library on mac) AND inconsistent (state -> ~/.local/state on mac). Fix: gate dirs::* fallback behind cfg(windows).

  config:  $XDG_CONFIG_HOME | ~/.config (unix incl mac) | %APPDATA% (win)
  data:    $XDG_DATA_HOME   | ~/.local/share            | %APPDATA% (win)
  state:   $XDG_STATE_HOME  | ~/.local/state            | %LOCALAPPDATA% (win)
  cache:   $XDG_CACHE_HOME  | ~/.cache                  | %LOCALAPPDATA% (win)

Audit verdicts: truly cross-platform already = agntz, ignr, skdlr, tmpltr, scrpr. Needs work = mmry (hard Windows compile blocker), eavs, trx, sx, byt, sldr (cosmetic). macOS already works for ALL tools; Windows is the only real frontier.

title: mmry: gate libc::_exit() for Windows compile
description: mmry-cli/src/main.rs:117 calls libc::_exit() unguarded -> won't COMPILE on Windows (only hard blocker in the ecosystem). Wrap in cfg(unix) with a std::process::exit fallback for non-unix. No macOS impact (compiles there today). CROSS-REPO: byteowlz/mmry.

title: Fix canonical paths helper in template (done) + one-time migrate existing copies
description:
XDG paths option-B migration (zero-dep). Template fixed (source of truth); oqto-stack + 6 extra repos migrated. All on per-repo branches, NOT pushed.

MIGRATED (branch / commit):
- templates 959423c, byt 769a088 (.6) | skdlr c51ad5b, ignr 8f9361d, trx 0b202c8 (.4), tmpltr 89f2bd6, sldr 1d18750 [fix/xdg-paths-zero-dep]
- sx 9459eab [fix/xdg-paths-windows] (.5) | eavs ff67bf1 [fix/xdg-paths-and-portable-paths] (.3) | mmry 30d255c [fix/xdg-paths-and-windows-compile] (.1)
- helmet 47f8075, gvnr 1921316, tmz 5e3e37f, dgrmr 2c67ee8, kyz a1d747f+e07e778 [fix/xdg-paths-zero-dep]
- agntz 243bf35 [chore/drop-unused-dirs] (unused dirs dropped). scrpr already correct.

RESOLVED this pass: kyz policy.rs residual fixed (e07e778, dirs fully gone); agntz unused-dirs cleanup done.

KNOWN (separate): gvnr workspace pre-broken on base (stale `rust_core` imports, unrelated). 

REMAINING non-stack repos (fix-when-touched; template covers new ones): castR, hdscl, lnkdn, ocr, persona, rlmz, rvrsd, twttr, whtspp, mailz, dgrmr-cli.

ALL branches need review + merge + push (cross-repo).

title: eavs: adopt paths helper + fix system template-discovery paths
description: eavs hand-rolls XDG (no dirs crate) and hardcodes /tmp, /usr/share/eavs, /opt/homebrew/..., /usr/local/share/eavs (provider_templates.rs:154-165; cli.rs:3129,4317). (a) Replace user-dir logic with the vendored helper. (b) Template discovery is a separate concern (install-location lookup) — derive from the binary's own dir or a documented, cfg-gated search order instead of hardcoded unix prefixes. CROSS-REPO: byteowlz/eavs. Blocked by the helper task.

title: trx: replace ~/.local/state fallback with paths helper
description: trx-core/src/service.rs:230 hardcodes dirs::home_dir().join('.local/state') with no Windows branch. Use the vendored helper's state_dir(). Rest of trx is well-gated (CommandExt/kill/taskkill + platform-aware help). CROSS-REPO: byteowlz/trx. Blocked by the helper task.

title: sx: add Windows config/state via Go paths equivalent
description: sx (Go) hardcodes ~/.config (config.go:106) and ~/.local/state (history.go:19) with no %APPDATA%/%LOCALAPPDATA% fallback (compiles for Windows via goreleaser but misplaces config). Implement the Go equivalent of the canonical rule (XDG_* wins; ~/.config on unix incl mac; APPDATA on win). scrpr already does this correctly - copy its pattern. CROSS-REPO: byteowlz/sx.

title: byt: adopt paths helper for state dir
description: byt src/main.rs:749 (get_state_dir) hardcodes ~/.local/state fallback, no Windows branch. Adopt the vendored helper. We are already on byt feat/byt-release. CROSS-REPO: byteowlz/byt. Blocked by the helper task.

title: sldr: cfg-guard hardcoded /usr/bin chromium candidates
description: sldr export.rs:870-871 lists /usr/bin/chromium, /usr/bin/google-chrome unguarded. Cosmetic (fails gracefully on Windows) - cfg-guard for tidiness. CROSS-REPO: byteowlz/sldr.

title: Fix list sessions query assuming oqto_log_sessions.title column
close_reason: Fixed: list/get-session queries probe pragma_table_info('oqto_log_sessions') for 'title' (ops.rs:526) and substitute 'NULL AS title' (:543) when absent. Verified.

title: mmry port allocation doesn't prevent orphan config collisions
description:
When users are deleted from the frontend but not from the OS, their mmry config files retain the port allocation. When the DB record is deleted, that port is freed in the DB but the orphaned mmry-service process still holds it. New users can then be allocated the same port via ensure_mmry_port (which only checks the DB), causing 'Address already in use' crash-loops.

Root cause: delete_user only removed from DB, not from OS. Fixed by adding full Linux cleanup to delete-user.

Additional concern: if usermgr provisioning writes the mmry config before the DB port is persisted, and provisioning fails midway, the config file retains a port that was never committed to the DB.

Consider: on runner startup, verify the mmry config port matches the DB-allocated port and fix mismatches.

title: Add remote runner capabilities (TCP transport) to oqto-runner with transport abstraction
description:
Context
- Oqto currently routes to per-user runners primarily via Unix sockets in local/systemd mode.
- Container-native multi-user runtime needs remote runner connectivity across container boundaries.
- We want one runner implementation with multiple transports, not separate runner products.

Goal
- Add TCP transport to existing oqto-runner while preserving Unix socket mode and protocol semantics.

Scope
1) Runner transport abstraction
   - Factor listener/connection handling behind a transport layer.
   - Support Unix socket (existing) and TCP listener (new) with identical request/response semantics.
2) CLI/config surface
   - Keep existing `--socket` behavior.
   - Add `--listen <host:port>` for TCP mode.
   - Enforce deny-by-default for TCP via runtime gate (e.g. env/config allowlist).
3) Authentication and security
   - Require auth token for TCP transport in v1.
   - Reject unauthenticated requests.
   - Document internal-network-only deployment posture.
4) Oqto backend integration
   - Add RunnerClient transport abstraction in backend.
   - Implement UnixRunnerClient and TcpRunnerClient.
   - Runtime mode selects transport (local/systemd => unix, container => tcp).
5) Compatibility and tests
   - Keep protocol payloads unchanged across transports.
   - Add transport parity tests and basic auth failure tests.

Event bus impact
- Runner protocol remains execution-plane internal.
- Oqto translates scheduler/runner lifecycle into canonical bus events:
  - scheduler.job.queued
  - scheduler.job.dispatched
  - scheduler.job.started
  - scheduler.job.completed
  - scheduler.job.failed
  - scheduler.job.retry_scheduled
- No frontend dependency on runner transport (unix/tcp is transparent to bus consumers).
- Existing agent stream stays unchanged; scheduler events flow on bus channel policy.

Acceptance criteria
- TCP runner works in container mode with auth token.
- Unix socket mode unchanged for local/systemd deployments.
- Backend can route jobs/sessions via either transport without protocol divergence.
- Bus events for job lifecycle emitted consistently regardless of transport.
- Security defaults prevent accidental TCP exposure in local mode.

title: Runner: add transport abstraction (unix + tcp listeners)

Runner: enforce TCP auth token validation for all requests
Backend: introduce RunnerClient interface with unix/tcp adapters
Runtime config: select runner transport by mode (local=unix, container=tcp)
Event bus: emit scheduler job lifecycle events independent of runner transport
Security: integrate kyz-backed runner token provisioning and rotation
Tests: transport parity + auth failure coverage for runner dispatch
Fix frontend session rename regression

title: Admin scripts: oqto-admin for server maintenance tasks
close_reason: Implemented in scripts/admin/. Covers eavs provisioning, pi config sync, skills management, template management, user status. Just recipes added.

title: P0: Assistant responses disappear from frontend despite existing in JSONL/hstry
Research agent-browser integration points and verify protocol compatibility
close_reason: agent-browser is already integrated in oqto (agent_browser.rs module exists)

title: Optional microvm isolation tier: separate guest kernel for kernel-LPE containment (KVM-gated, fail-closed)
description: Add a fourth runtime tier (local/runner/container/microvm) giving a separate-kernel boundary for untrusted or high-value workloads. Key decisions from 2026-06-09 evaluation (docs/active/design/20260609-isolation-tiers-and-egress.md): (1) per-USER microVM (runner + that user's pi sessions inside one VM), not per-session; (2) prototype with gondolin krun backend (virtiofs workspace mount + egress hooks exist today; treat as spike, v0.12 single-vendor, not a prod dependency); (3) productionize on Cloud Hypervisor (Rust VMM, virtiofs, Kata default backend) or Kata-on-CH via the container tier - NOT Firecracker (no virtiofs by design, block-device-only storage fights workspace model); (4) run oqto-sandbox INSIDE the guest (defense in depth); (5) capability detection: probe /dev/kvm at runner startup, tier unavailable without it; per-workspace policy may REQUIRE microvm -> fail closed, never silently downgrade; KVM-capable runner subset serves sensitive workspaces (remote-runner routing). Rationale: only a VM makes 'all egress through the injector' and kernel-LPE containment structural guarantees; netns/nftables level-2 capture holds only up to the shared kernel.

title: Fix deploy remediation for skdlr when release URL or rustup default is missing
close_reason: Fixed: deploy.sh sources cargo env + 'rustup default stable' (:578) before cargo fallback (commit 1a52c1d1); skdlr source path fix (commit 6a5816e0); missing release URL fails closed. Verified.

title: oqto-usermgr writes stale [external_api] enable field; mmry 0.10.0 renamed to enabled
description:
oqto-usermgr/src/main.rs:963-967 writes `[external_api] enable = true` into per-user `~/.config/mmry/config.toml`.

mmry renamed this field from `enable` to `enabled` in 0.10.0 (trx mmry-ypv4, closed 2026-03-03), to align with every other config section (`service.enabled`, `embeddings.enabled`, `analyzer.enabled`).

Impact: on a fresh mmry install for a new oqto user, the external API config silently falls back to the default for `enabled` (false), so the HTTP endpoint oqto proxies to never comes up. Existing users with already-written configs continue working only because mmry tolerates the stale field via deserialization defaults — but new provisioning is broken.

Fix: change `enable = true` to `enabled = true` in the format! string at oqto-usermgr/src/main.rs:964.

Also worth a quick sweep for any other `[external_api] enable` / `console_enable` occurrences in deploy scripts and setup tooling.

title: backend Cargo.toml uses worktree-hostile relative path dep mmry-core = ../../mmry/crates/mmry-core
description: Introduced by 47cfa9c6. The relative path '../../mmry/crates/mmry-core' resolves only from the canonical repo location (byteowlz/mmry). From a git worktree under .claude/worktrees/<name>/backend, ../../ resolves to .claude/worktrees/, so cargo fails: 'failed to load manifest for dependency mmry-core: No such file or directory' -- breaking ALL cargo commands in any worktree, and likely CI/deploy checkouts not located as a sibling of mmry. Fix options: (a) vendor/publish mmry-core, (b) use a git dependency instead of path, (c) make path resolution location-independent. Workaround for now: symlink the mmry repo to the expected relative location.
close_reason: Fixed by commit 56f2f45b: backend/Cargo.toml:111 uses mmry-core = { git = .../mmry, branch = main }; the worktree-hostile ../../mmry relative path is gone (oqto/Cargo.toml:123 inherits via workspace).

title: Fix hstry adapter provisioning drift for new users
description:
Incident: account setup failed with 'Account setup is taking longer...' because oqto-usermgr provisions new users with hstry adapter_paths=["/usr/local/share/hstry/adapters"], and that global adapter bundle drifted to hstry_version 0.5.12 while binary expected 0.5.15. Existing users were patched manually, but new users kept failing until /usr/local/share/hstry/adapters was refreshed.

Root cause
- backend/crates/oqto-usermgr/src/main.rs writes/patches config to use only /usr/local/share/hstry/adapters.
- No deploy/setup guard guarantees that global adapter bundle version matches installed hstry binary.

Required fix (durable)
1) Provisioning: prefer per-user adapter path first (e.g. /home/<user>/.config/hstry/adapters), keep global as fallback.
2) During setup-user-runner, run Updated adapters in /home/wismut/.config/hstry/adapters
  official: 14 adapters as target user before starting hstry.service (or on mismatch self-heal path).
3) Deploy/setup script: add explicit global adapter sync/verification step and fail fast if mismatch persists.
4) Add health check/assertion in oqto-usermgr logs: compare expected vs found adapter version and print actionable remediation.

Acceptance criteria
- Fresh signup works after hstry binary upgrade with no manual intervention.
- New user hstry starts on first attempt; no adapter mismatch loops.
- Deploy pipeline includes adapter compatibility check.
- Regression test or scripted repro included.

title: Runner uses stale/unreachable hstry gRPC endpoint causing message persist drops
description: Runner hstry persist failures were caused by stale ~/.local/state/hstry/service.port (43381) while active systemd-managed hstry listened on 33431. HstryClient trusted port-file endpoint and failed with connection refused, causing dropped persistence/client_id updates. Fixed by adding robust endpoint fallback in HstryClient: try port-file endpoint, then fallback to 33431, and also attempt fallback when port file missing. Improved HstryServiceManager is_running health check to verify actual endpoint connectivity and include 33431 fallback instead of file-existence only. Verified by reproducing send in real-hero-pest session; connection-refused warnings disappeared after endpoint correction.
close_reason: HstryClient now uses explicit per-user endpoints via HstryEndpoint enum. No more global hardcoded port fallback. Runner resolves endpoint at startup from socket/port-file with connectivity verification.

title: Session rename reverts: update_chat_session returns external_id while list returns platform_id
description: Root cause expanded: manual rename only updated hstry; Pi JSONL session_info remained stale when session was not live/ready. On next message/get_state sync, stale Pi title overwrote hstry, causing rename reversion. Fix in progress: runner update_workspace_chat_session now attempts live Pi set_session_name and falls back to appending session_info directly to JSONL by external_id when session is inactive.

title: Standardize byteowlz release packaging; collapse oqto-setup acquire to one scheme
description:
Standardize release packaging across all byteowlz apps so oqto-setup acquire collapses to one rule and drift is impossible. Decision: docs/adr/0021-standard-release-packaging-byteowlz-apps.md.

Spec: artifact `{name}-v{ver}-{target-triple}.tar.gz` (Rust + Go), mandatory checksums.txt, executables under bin/, target matrix incl aarch64 + darwin/windows where supported, v{semver} tags.

Where: implemented once as `byt release` (cross-repo meta-tool), invoked by a thin reusable workflow in byteowlz/.github; consumer repos call it pinned to a tag.

Org-level: oqto is the driving consumer; byt/.github/per-repo work is cross-repo (track in those repos' trackers too).

title: Author ADR-0021 release-packaging spec
description: Define the byteowlz release spec + where it's enforced. docs/adr/0021-standard-release-packaging-byteowlz-apps.md.

title: skdlr: publish checksums.txt in its release (unblocks full acquire)
description: skdlr ships release tarballs but no checksums.txt, so oqto-setup acquire fail-closes on it (10/11 tools verify; skdlr is the lone outlier). Quick fix in skdlr's release workflow. CROSS-REPO (byteowlz/skdlr). Independent of the broader rollout.

title: Distribute byt (GHCR image) + implement byt release + reusable .github workflow
description:
Enforcement half of ADR-0021. Implemented as ordered sub-steps; CROSS-REPO (byteowlz/byt + byteowlz/.github). Decisions: docs/adr/0021 (spec) + docs/adr/0022 (byt distribution).

Sub-steps (in order):
1. Decide+bootstrap byt distribution (ADR-0022): build container image ghcr.io/byteowlz/byt bundling byt + cargo-zigbuild/zig + rust + go, from source ONCE (one-off byt-repo workflow or local docker build), push to GHCR (private; pulled from same-org Actions via GITHUB_TOKEN). byt is private with 0 releases today, so this is the unlock.
   Interim option to prove the flow before the image exists: cargo install --git (with token) on a rust runner.
2. Implement the `byt release` subcommand per ADR-0021:
   - subcommands: build [--target T] / checksums / publish [--tag] / verify <dir|url> / all-in-one; --dry-run everywhere.
   - per-repo byt.release.toml: name, bins[], targets[], extra[], sign, lang.
   - delegate ONLY compile to language backends (cargo-zigbuild for Rust, go for Go); byt owns staging (top-level {name}-v{ver}-{triple}/ dir with bin/ + LICENSE/README), tar.gz naming, checksums.txt, optional signing, gh release publish.
   - after first image, byt self-hosts its own releases.
3. Reusable workflow byteowlz/.github/.github/workflows/release.yml: job runs `container: ghcr.io/byteowlz/byt:vN`, checkout, `byt release` (GITHUB_TOKEN). Consumer repos call it pinned to a tag (~3-line release.yml).

Caveat: container: jobs are Linux-only -> covers required linux x86_64/aarch64 (zigbuild). Native darwin/windows need a prebuilt byt binary via a byteowlz/setup-byt action; defer until wanted.

Done when: a tag push on a conforming repo produces spec-shaped artifacts (oqto-setup acquire verifies them end-to-end).

title: Migrate each byteowlz repo's release.yml to the reusable workflow
description: Per-repo: replace bespoke release config with a thin caller of the byteowlz/.github reusable workflow, pinned to a tag. Repos: oqto, eavs, mmry, trx, agntz, sx, scrpr, tmpltr, sldr, skdlr, ignr. CROSS-REPO. Blocked on .3.

title: Collapse oqto-setup deps.rs to one naming scheme + uniform bin/ placement
description: Once repos conform to ADR-0021: delete classify()/ArtifactNaming variants + Oqto special case in oqto-setup/src/deps.rs; make extract->PATH a uniform 'install bin/*' step. oqto-side cleanup. Blocked on .4.

title: Runner: transactional fork state machine (single in-flight fork per session)
oqto-sandbox seccomp Audit mode silently enforces (no SCMP_ACT_LOG variant); sibling of oqto-2eev landlock audit bug
Migrate from oqto-browser to agent-browser
Fix just update-deps sed expression causing pre-commit failure
close_reason: Fixed malformed sed replacement in just update-deps; validated command runs successfully without sed parse errors.

title: Frontend comprehensive refactor
description:
Comprehensive frontend refactor to improve performance, maintainability, and code quality.

## Motivation
- Lighthouse performance score: 52/100
- Time to Interactive: 160.6s, Total Blocking Time: 6,250ms
- 246 useEffect calls across 84 files (most are avoidable)
- God components: ChatView.tsx (4,133 lines, 27 effects), AppShellRoute.tsx (1,337 lines, 13 effects), CanvasView.tsx (2,643 lines, 11 effects)
- 87,402 lines of TypeScript across 349 files
- ~2.9MB unused JS on initial load

## Phases
1. **Foundation** -- Lint rules, hooks, patterns (blocks nothing, prevents new debt)
2. **Architecture** -- Decompose god components, extract shared patterns
3. **Data layer** -- Consistent TanStack Query usage, remove fetch-in-effect
4. **Performance** -- Code splitting, lazy loading, bundle optimization
5. **Cleanup** -- useEffect ban enforcement, final lint pass

## Principles
- No useEffect (use useMountEffect for external sync)
- Derived state computed inline, never synced via effects
- Data fetching via TanStack Query exclusively
- Components under 300 lines, single responsibility
- Event-driven logic in handlers, not effects

title: Align login form inputs with sidebar search style
close_reason: Updated LoginPage inputs to use sidebar-style thin grey border, removed input shadow and focus ring halo via class overrides.

title: Repo cleanup: remove orphaned deploy methods + dead scripts; mark superseded docs
description: Verified-deadweight sweep (read-only audit + per-item re-grep). Tier A deletes (0 active refs): deploy/{archiso,lima,launchd}, 11 orphaned top-level scripts. Tier C: SUPERSEDED-BY-ADR headers on design docs 20260313/20260508, narrowed-note on 20260314. Held (NOT deleted): frontend/mini-apps (WIP), CLAUDE.md (harness symlink), oqto-guard (wired into prompts), update-deps-precommit (1 ref), mmry migration scripts (active per deploy). Tier B (container/backend dead code) stays in the decomposition epic oqto-3ct7 + ADR-0001, not ad-hoc.
close_reason: Tier A: deleted deploy/{archiso,lima,launchd} + 11 orphaned scripts (each verified 0 active refs). Tier C: superseded/narrowed headers on 3 design docs. Kept per judgment: CLAUDE.md symlink, oqto-guard (prompts-wired), mini-apps (WIP). Tier B (container/backend dead code) remains in oqto-3ct7 + ADR-0001.

title: Mobile: fork icon tap does nothing in chat message actions
Deploy-time history cutover: fully converge chat history into oqto-log and remove runtime fallback gaps

title: Pi does not recover from hung LLM streams (no stream timeout)
description: When an upstream LLM provider (e.g. Kimi-K2.5) drops a streaming connection mid-response without sending [DONE] or an error, Pi hangs indefinitely in its event loop. The TCP connection closes but Pi never detects the stream ended. Observed on octo-azure where Kimi dropped reasoning_content mid-token. Pi should implement a stream inactivity timeout (e.g. 120s with no chunks) and surface it as an error so the user can retry.

title: Fold seccomp BPF install into deploy.sh (stage+install atomically instead of preflight-check only)
close_reason: deploy.sh now stages+installs seccomp BPF atomically. prepare_host ships both arch artifacts under <release>/seccomp/; new install_seccomp_bpf() helper installs the host-arch BPF to /usr/local/share/oqto/seccomp/default.bpf (+/etc symlink) during activate_host and restores prior policy on rollback_host. Preflight changed to gate on source-artifact availability (avoids first-deploy deadlock). Updated deploy/DEPLOY.md. Validated: bash -n clean; fail-closed under set -euo pipefail; dry-run branches present. End-to-end exercised on next real deploy/--dry-run.

Deploy SearXNG on octo-azure and configure sx for all users

title: Replace mmry service integration with embedded mmry-core memory file
description:
Implemented workspace memory cutover to mmry-core JSONL and removed key per-user mmry service dependencies. Added deploy/update migration path using Python migration script (based on ../mmry/scripts/migrate_legacy_mmry_to_jsonl.py):
- New scripts/migrate-mmry-jsonl.sh migrates legacy SQLite stores into <workspace>/.mmry/mmry.jsonl for single-user and multi-user modes
- Added scripts/migrate_legacy_mmry_to_jsonl.py copy for deploy/update availability
- scripts/deploy.sh now runs migration step in activate flow before service restart (mode-aware)
- scripts/setup/12-update.sh now runs migration step during --update

Migration behavior is idempotent: only migrates workspaces without existing non-empty .mmry/mmry.jsonl; chooses legacy DB by repo/workspace/default store names.

title: Sandbox v2 hardening program (runner-first, remote-ready)
description:
Unify and execute Oqto sandbox hardening as a single program for local + remote runners.

Goals
- Defense-in-depth isolation in oqto-sandbox (not just bwrap).
- Runner-owned sandbox orchestration with fail-closed behavior.
- Secret isolation integrated with sandbox/network policy for remote runners.
- Production docs/tests/observability for operations.

Scope
1) Linux hardening baseline
   - no_new_privs
   - capability drop
   - seccomp-bpf default-deny allowlist
   - landlock layer where available
   - cgroup resource limits + kill semantics
2) Policy/config hardening
   - system read-only global sandbox config
   - deterministic workspace restriction merge
   - explicit proxy-only network mode for secret flows
3) Secret isolation integration
   - runner-side credential proxy / placeholder substitution
   - host allowlist pinning and audit logs
   - approval workflow + process allowlist
4) Verification
   - sandbox escape regression suite
   - secret-injection test matrix
   - docs + runbooks + telemetry

Included open issues
- oqto-cxxr
- octo-1ddx
- oqto-3c42
- oqto-cv3f
- oqto-ctpz
- oqto-dv37
- oqto-05c1
- oqto-8kx4
- oqto-q5yb
- oqto-xf80
- oqto-g3wq

Definition of done
- strict profile enforces seccomp + cap-drop + no_new_privs in CI-tested paths
- remote runner path enforces secret-safe egress via runner mediation
- documented threat model and operational playbook shipped
- all listed child issues closed or explicitly descoped with rationale

title: P1: Normalize malformed hstry content blobs (multi JSON arrays) to canonical parts
close_reason: Backend now repairs malformed multi-array JSON content blobs into canonical parts on read paths; added regression coverage.

title: Media Browsing & Discovery Improvements
description: Comprehensive overhaul of media file browsing in Oqto: real thumbnails, quick filters, video preview, gallery view, and batch operations. Implement backend thumbnail generation, UI improvements, and full media browsing experience.
close_reason: All media browsing features implemented: backend thumbnail generation (images + video via ffmpeg), frontend thumbnail display with progressive loading, media quick access bar with type filters and search, inline video preview, lightbox gallery with filmstrip navigation, batch operations, hover video preview.

title: Runner-only personal path: LISTEN_FDS + systemd socket-activated template units
description: Per ADR-0002. Runner accepts an inherited listener (LISTEN_FDS); ship oqto-runner@{user}.socket/.service at /run/oqto/runner-sockets/{user}/. Deletes backend revival logic (ensure_runner_healthy, usermgr reach-back) and the socket-path divergence. macOS/personal-dev exempt: backend supervises a same-uid child runner. This is the gate for deleting local mode (oqto-3ct7.15).

title: Remove ttyd TCP fallback and enforce unix-socket-only terminal transport
Fix tool summary mislabeling sx commands as file reads when piped to head
close_reason: Fixed by commit fcf85286: file-read detection anchored to primaryCommandSegment (frontend/lib/tool-summaries.ts:33, used at :191) so 'sx ... | head' isn't mislabeled; regression test tool-summaries.test.ts:61. Verified.

title: oqto-egress-shim: in-namespace PROXY-v2 forwarder + re-point DNAT to local shim
description:
Completes Option C for NetworkMode::Proxy egress (eavs side done: eavs-frd8 transparent listener + DNS relay). oqto-sandbox needs the in-namespace shim that conveys the real destination to eavs.

Build oqto-egress-shim (Rust, thread-per-conn + splice, no tokio, ~1-2MB RSS):
- listen in the agent netns on a local addr (where the netns DNAT now points)
- getsockopt SO_ORIGINAL_DST (works in-netns where the DNAT/conntrack live) -> exact orig dst IP:port
- connect eavs at host_ip:<transparent_port> over the veth
- write PROXY protocol v2 header (TCP4/TCP6) carrying the orig dst, then splice
- pure mechanism: NO ACL/secrets (those live in eavs)

oqto-sandbox egress.rs changes:
- re-point the nft DNAT from host_ip:tcp_port to the LOCAL shim addr in the netns (keeps part-1 per-netns topology)
- DNS: DNAT udp 53 -> host_ip:dns_port (eavs DNS relay) -- or via shim
- spawn the shim in the netns (setns) as part of prepare_egress/EgressGuard; kill on teardown (extend the guard to own the shim child)
- config: thread eavs transparent host/port into the egress plan (proxy_tcp_port already exists; add the eavs host)

Then end-to-end test on a privileged host: agent in proxy mode resolves via DNS relay, connects allowlisted domain (spliced) and a denied domain (blocked). Monitor vs enforce posture honored by eavs. Depends on eavs branch feat/transparent-egress-proxy.
close_reason: Implemented as oqto-egress-relay (renamed from 'shim' to avoid collision with the landlock shim). Built across commits on branch worktree-netmode-proxy-egress: shim.rs->landlock_shim.rs rename; oqto-egress-relay binary + egress_relay.rs (PROXY v2 writer, binary resolver); egress.rs nft DNAT re-pointed to in-namespace relay (guest:10000), DNS->host:dns_port; relay spawned via setns in prepare_egress and owned by EgressGuard (killed on teardown, fail-closed). Validated on live kernel via unprivileged userns: new ruleset loads + SO_ORIGINAL_DST recovers the true dst through the DNAT (RELAY_RECOVERED 198.51.100.7:443). eavs side complete on branch feat/transparent-egress-proxy. REMAINING (ops, not code): deploy the oqto-egress-relay binary; end-to-end validation on a privileged host (agent proxy session through eavs); merge both branches. MITM/credential injection stays eavs-sth0.

title: Add Rust AI guardrail linting baseline + ast-grep integration
close_reason: Implemented Rust AI guardrail baseline with ast-grep, reusable reporting, production-scope CI enforcement (tests excluded), and documented policy in AGENTS.md

title: Capability-based harness feature negotiation (shell escape, compaction, fork, etc.)
description: Runner should advertise harness capabilities in handshake (e.g. shell_escape, bash_command, compaction, fork, model_switch). Frontend checks capabilities and uses native harness features when available (e.g. ! prefix routes to Pi bash RPC command). Falls back to instruction-based prompting when harness lacks the capability. First use case: shell escape (! and !!) in chat input using Pi's native bash command instead of instructing the agent. Pattern applies to all harness-specific features for harness-agnostic frontend.

title: Activity heartbeats: sandbox allow_write + per-principal dir + pi emitter
description: Per ADR-0013. (1) Add the activity dir to sandbox allow_write (XDG_STATE_HOME/agent-activity is NOT writable in the development profile today - blocker). (2) Per-principal 0700 dir in multi-user (host-visible, no cross-read). (3) pi extension writes file-first heartbeats keyed by PLATFORM_SESSION_ID; payload mirrors the gvnr event schema. gvnr owns the convention (var/default path/file schema/expiry); reader does heartbeat-age expiry; commands redacted, message previews opt-in. Depends on AGENT_CTX injection.

title: Use EAVS API for provider/model CRUD from Oqto (no direct models.json drift)
description:
Problem: Oqto frontend provider/model additions currently update Pi models.json only and do not update EAVS global provider config. This causes drift: provisioned eavs-* catalog and user-edited catalogs diverge. Expected: adding provider/model should integrate with EAVS control-plane so global routing source-of-truth stays in EAVS.

Proposal:
1) Add Oqto admin flow: create/update/delete providers/models via EAVS API/CLI (not by editing config files directly).
2) After successful mutation, trigger EAVS reload/sync and regenerate per-user models.json from EAVS /providers/detail.
3) Keep user/workspace .oqto/models.json as optional overlay/restriction layer, but base catalog comes from EAVS.
4) Add clear UI distinction: Global Provider (EAVS) vs Workspace Catalog Override (.oqto/models.json).

Acceptance:
- New provider added in UI appears in /providers/detail immediately
- sync-models propagates to all users
- no manual edits to /home/oqto/.config/eavs/config.toml required
- no key material stored in workspace catalogs

title: oqtoctl user management: set-password, disable/enable, set-role, sessions
close_reason: Implemented set-password, set-role, disable, enable, set-email, set-display-name, reprovision commands in oqtoctl. Backend PUT /admin/users/{id} already existed.

title: Mobile chat: inline media taps open Copy All context menu instead of interacting with image/video
description:
On touch devices, chat bubbles and text segments are wrapped in Radix ContextMenuTrigger for copy actions. This can hijack long-press/tap events on inline @file previews. Symptoms: inline image preview intermittently fails to open/render on mobile; inline video controls trigger the copy-all modal instead of playback.

Fix implemented:
- frontend/features/chat/components/ChatView.tsx
  - Added useCoarsePointerDevice() via matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').
  - In MessageGroupCard, skip ContextMenu wrapper on coarse-pointer devices.
  - In TextWithFileReferences, skip ContextMenu wrapper on coarse-pointer devices.
  - Keep desktop right-click copy behavior unchanged.

Validation notes:
- Local desktop automation cannot faithfully emulate coarse-pointer long-press behavior, so final confirmation should be from real mobile device in workspace cmfy.

title: Render only one retry card per assistant turn
close_reason: Deduplicated retrying error segments in MessageGroupCard so only latest retry card renders per assistant group.

title: Follow-up user messages disappear from frontend timeline
close_reason: Authoritative merge now preserves recent optimistic user messages (2min TTL) so follow-up messages stay visible even when hstry persist fails.

title: Scope oqto-log identity import to configured workspace root
close_reason: Deploy identity sync now derives the configured workspace root, imports only matching Pi JSONL workspaces, cleans out-of-scope identity-only rows, and chat history requests query the allowed root instead of all workspaces

title: Hover Video Preview
description: Hover over video thumbnail for 1-2s to auto-play muted preview (first 5-10s on loop). Click to unmute/open full player. Great for scanning multiple videos.
close_reason: Hover 800ms on video thumbnail for muted loop preview. Auto-stops on mouse leave.

title: Scalability: Protocol versioning for canonical protocol evolution
description:
The canonical protocol (docs/design/canonical-protocol.md) currently has no versioning mechanism. Adding new Part types, Event variants, or Command payloads will break older runners or frontends. This blocks safe protocol evolution.

## Requirements

1. Version Negotiation
   - runner.hello includes supported protocol versions
   - Backend selects common version or rejects connection
   - Frontend includes version in WebSocket handshake

2. Backward Compatibility Strategy
   - Minor versions: additive changes only (new optional fields)
   - Major versions: breaking changes (new required fields, renamed types)
   - Version-specific serialization adapters

3. Deprecation Warnings
   - Backend logs when old versions connect
   - Admin tooling to identify outdated runners
   - Migration timeline for version sunset

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Protocol version in all handshake messages
- [ ] Backend rejects incompatible versions with clear error
- [ ] New fields gracefully ignored by old consumers
- [ ] Version matrix documented
- [ ] CI testing for N and N-1 version compatibility

## Related
- canonical-protocol.md Part type definitions
- Runner registration protocol
- WebSocket multiplexed channel handshake

title: Spawn-time auto-injection engine in runner
Rehome oqto-ssh-proxy under the runner (keep, do not delete)
description: Per ADR-0007. The 461-line SSH-agent-protocol proxy is real and needed (SSH cannot be SNI/Host-rewritten). Resolve 'wire or delete' to WIRE: runner spawns it per session, sets SSH_AUTH_SOCK to its socket, supplies policy + approval channel (runner-mediated, replacing the legacy direct-to-backend --oqto-server prompt). Upstream becomes the configured SecretProvider, not the ambient ssh-agent.

title: Add message sync test suite: unit + integration + E2E tests for Pi->hstry->Frontend pipeline
description:
Create comprehensive test coverage for the message sync pipeline to prevent regressions.

Unit tests (Rust):
- T1: PiTranslator produces output for all PiEvent variants
- T2: AgentMessage -> proto Message -> SerializableMessage round-trip preserves content
- T3: Message indices are monotonically increasing across persist calls
- T4: parse_all handles 1-10 concatenated JSON objects correctly
- T5: Verify stdout_reader uses parse_all not parse (code-level invariant)
- T6: Compaction guard: persist does NOT overwrite when Pi has fewer messages than hstry

Integration tests (Rust):
- T7: Full pipeline: mock Pi events -> hstry persist -> canonical events emitted
- T8: Broadcast overflow triggers resync_required
- T9: Version vector increments on every hstry mutation

E2E tests (TypeScript/browser):
- E2E-1: Every Pi response appears on frontend under normal conditions
- E2E-2: Messages survive WebSocket reconnect mid-stream
- E2E-3: Session switch loads complete history
- E2E-4: Frontend message count matches hstry after every agent.idle
- E2E-5: Compaction preserves all messages on frontend
- E2E-6: JSONL message count >= hstry message count (health check script)

See: docs/design/message-sync-invariants.md section 6 for full test specifications.

title: Shared workspace sessions: frontend creates new session ID instead of reusing existing hstry session ID
description:
When a user navigates to a shared workspace session (e.g. nimble-tests-ladder with session ID f3ce6d6b), the frontend:
1. Fetches history using the correct ID (f3ce6d6b) -> gets 101 messages
2. But creates a FRESH oqto-UUID for session.create (e.g. oqto-7d09d46b)
3. Runner spawns a brand new Pi process for oqto-7d09d46b
4. Prompt goes to the new Pi (empty context)
5. Events flow back on oqto-7d09d46b but frontend display is confused
6. History and active session are on different IDs

Root cause: createPiSessionId() always generates a new oqto- UUID. For sessions that already exist in list_sessions, the frontend should reuse the existing session_id (or hstry_id).

This only affects shared workspace sessions because personal sessions maintain session state across page loads via the session sidebar.
close_reason: State-machine hardening done: no implicit session ID creation during reconnect/session drift; reconnect uses single in-flight reconcile transaction; backend fails closed for synthetic fork entry IDs (history-*).
