title: Persistence hardening: deterministic message timeline tests across frontend, runner, and e2e
description:
Goal
Eliminate recurring frontend message persistence regressions (disappearing/duplicated/out-of-order messages) by building a full regression net that validates live streaming, reconciliation, persistence, and reload/reconnect convergence.

Context
Recent fixes addressed many individual defects, but regressions still recur due to event interleavings across:
- frontend merge modes (partial vs authoritative)
- runner live buffer and session lifecycle
- durability boundaries (hstry/oqto-log)
- reconnect/session-switch races

Work breakdown
- oqto-zpvs.1 Frontend deterministic event-interleaving tests
- oqto-zpvs.2 Frontend property-style invariant tests for merge/normalize
- oqto-zpvs.3 Frontend trace replay harness with historical incident fixtures
- oqto-zpvs.4 Backend integration tests for persist-before-idle + fetch contracts
- oqto-zpvs.5 Backend integration tests for session identity mapping/isolation
- oqto-zpvs.6 E2E chaos scenarios (reconnect/switch/retry/fork/reload)
- oqto-zpvs.7 CI gate + observability + triage tooling

Global invariants (must hold in all test layers)
- No duplicate logical turns (id/client_id reconciliation correctness)
- No message disappearance once rendered unless superseded by authoritative persisted message
- Stable append-only ordering for completed turns
- Convergence: final UI timeline equals authoritative persisted timeline after settle
- Cross-session isolation: events/messages never leak between sessions

Definition of done
- Child issues completed
- New suites running in CI with documented commands
- At least 10 historical regression traces encoded as automated tests
- Runbook for triaging failures (frontend/runner/store evidence flow)

title: Frontend: add deterministic event-interleaving reducer tests for message convergence
description:
Problem
Current frontend tests cover many isolated cases but not broad interleavings that trigger live regressions.

Deliverables
- Add a deterministic interleaving harness for chat event sequences (stream/tool/agent/messages/response).
- Generate permutation suites for key race windows:
  - stream.done -> agent.idle gap
  - partial messages snapshot arriving during sending/streaming/syncing
  - reconnect resync + stale fetch discard
  - session switch while async history fetch is in-flight
- Assert invariants at every step and at convergence.

Files (likely)
- frontend/tests/message-interleaving-regression.test.ts
- frontend/features/chat/hooks/message-utils.ts (helpers only if required)
- frontend/features/chat/hooks/chat-state-machine.ts (test helpers if required)

Acceptance criteria
- >= 25 interleaving cases, deterministic and non-flaky
- Each case validates: no dup logical turns, no disappearance of unsuperseded messages, stable ordering
- Existing dedup and buffer tests still pass

Validation
- cd frontend && bun run test -- message-interleaving-regression
- cd frontend && bun run test
close_reason: Added deterministic frontend interleaving harness in frontend/tests/message-interleaving-regression.test.ts with 27 non-flaky cases covering stream.done->idle gap, partial snapshots during sending/streaming/syncing, reconnect stale-fetch discard by epoch, and session-switch in-flight fetch discard by session. Added stepwise invariants (no duplicate ids/logical client turns, no unexpected partial drops, stable ordering) and convergence assertions. Validation: bun run test -- message-interleaving-regression; bun run test (25 files, 134 tests)

title: Fix duplicate optimistic user prompt tail by preserving client_id in authoritative timeline
description:
Problem
After removing heuristic text dedup, optimistic user prompts can duplicate: persisted copy appears in-place and unmatched optimistic copy remains and drifts to tail in authoritative merges.

Expected
No heuristics. Reconciliation must use stable identities only (client_id/id), and optimistic copy must collapse when persisted counterpart arrives.

Likely root cause
client_id is not consistently present on authoritative persisted user messages, so merge cannot match optimistic local clientId.

Deliverables
1) Trace and fix identity pipeline so user client_id survives to authoritative fetch path.
2) Keep merge logic heuristic-free.
3) Add regression test for 'duplicate optimistic user ends up at tail' scenario.

Validation
- backend targeted tests for message conversion/projection as needed
- frontend: bun run test -- dedup trace-replay response-command-dispatch
- frontend lint
close_reason: Root cause fixed: authoritative projection path could lose optimistic identity because client_id extraction only recognized snake_case () and ignored camelCase () / fallback payload variants. This prevented deterministic clientId reconciliation and left unmatched optimistic user turns appended at tail. Implemented non-heuristic identity fix by centralizing client-id extraction (snake+camel) in runner protocol, applying it in agent->chat proto conversion and hstry persist conversion, and adding oqto-log projector payload fallback extraction from JSON payloads. Added backend regression tests for snake/camel extraction and projector payload extraction; kept frontend merge heuristic-free. Validated backend tests (client_id extraction, persistence_contracts, session_identity_isolation), frontend tests (dedup/trace-replay/response-command-dispatch), and frontend lint.

title: Hotfix: enforce streaming tail anchor and ignore live snapshots during active turn
description:
Urgent follow-up fix for severe UX corruption: streaming content rendered at random historical positions and live snapshot replay duplicated parts.

Fix scope
- Streaming deltas must never attach to historical assistant messages. Ensure a dedicated tail container is always used for active streaming turns.
- While a live turn is active, ignore get_messages snapshots (including source=live) to prevent replaying partial windows over active delta stream.
- Keep authoritative reconciliation on idle.

Validation
- frontend tests: response-command-dispatch, dedup, trace-replay
- frontend lint
close_reason: Implemented tail-anchor invariant in useChat ensureAssistantMessage (streaming always uses dedicated tail container; never writes into historical assistant message). Restored strict guard in response-command-dispatch: ignore snapshot get_messages while live turn active to prevent replay/duplicate part corruption. Added regression test for ignoring live snapshots during active turn. Validated via bun test (response-command-dispatch/dedup/trace-replay) and bun lint.

title: Fix chat auto-scroll flicker at bottom during streaming
description:
Regression: after streaming/tail-anchor hotfix, auto scroll-to-bottom flickers while new deltas arrive.

Expected
- If user is pinned to bottom, scrolling remains smooth and monotonic without visible bounce/flicker.
- If user scrolled up, no forced jump.

Deliverables
- isolate root cause in ChatView/useChat scroll behavior
- implement stable bottom-anchor logic (single writer, no competing smooth/instant writes)
- add/adjust regression coverage if feasible

Validation
- frontend targeted tests and lint
close_reason: Fixed chat bottom-scroll flicker by stabilizing auto-scroll writer in ChatView: replaced double-write scrollToBottom (immediate + next-frame) with single rAF-coalesced bottom write and no-op when already at bottom. This removes programmatic scroll-event churn that caused visible flicker while streaming updates arrived. Validated with frontend tests (response-command-dispatch/dedup/trace-replay) and frontend lint.

title: Add optimistic bottom padding for live container during active turn
description:
Improve streaming scroll smoothness: while a live assistant turn is active, keep an optimistic bottom spacer so incremental line growth does not feel twitchy. Remove spacer on agent end/idle.

Deliverables
- Implement bottom padding/spacer behavior in chat viewport tied to active turn state.
- Ensure no layout jump on idle finalize.
- Keep auto-scroll semantics intact (no forced jumps when user scrolled up).

Validation
- frontend tests and lint
close_reason: Implemented optimistic live bottom spacer in ChatView: while live turn is active (streaming/awaiting/send pending), timeline includes a dedicated bottom spacer (56px) with short height transition; spacer collapses on end/idle. This keeps line-by-line growth smooth and avoids twitchy bottom chasing while preserving user-scrolled-up behavior. Validated via frontend tests (response-command-dispatch/trace-replay) and frontend lint.

title: Adjust live padding to inner message margins (not outer spacer)
description:
Follow-up UX fix: live padding should affect the inner text container where lines render, not an outer spacer block at timeline end.

Deliverables
- remove outer bottom spacer element
- apply dynamic bottom padding on inner messages wrapper during active live turn
- preserve smooth transition and bottom-scroll behavior

Validation
- frontend targeted tests + lint
close_reason: Adjusted live padding placement to inner message container (where text lines render): removed outer spacer and applied dynamic bottom padding on inner timeline wrapper during active live turns. Preserved smooth transition and existing scroll behavior. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch) and frontend lint.

title: Place streaming breathing room inside assistant block (not below container)
description:
Follow-up UI correction: live breathing room must be inside the assistant message block. Outer container should still extend to bottom as before.

Deliverables
- remove timeline-level bottom padding for live mode
- apply conditional bottom padding inside the active/live assistant MessageGroupCard content block
- ensure no gap appears below message container

Validation
- frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Moved live breathing room to inside assistant block content: removed timeline-level live padding, added conditional inner bottom padding on MessageGroupCard body for active last assistant group. This keeps the message container flush to bottom while giving streamed lines internal room. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch) and lint.

title: Reduce streaming twitchiness with stable timeline rows and scroll-owner hardening
description:
Follow-up after live padding placement. Keep live markdown rendering but reduce visual twitching.

Deliverables
- harden scroll owner semantics (disable browser scroll anchoring on chat timeline where manual bottom-follow is used)
- stabilize grouped timeline row identity so unchanged groups do not rerender on every streaming delta
- keep streaming strictly tail-anchored and preserve user-scrolled-up behavior

Validation
- frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch) + lint
close_reason: Applied anti-twitch hardening while keeping live markdown: (1) stabilized message-group identity across streaming deltas using structural sharing (reuse unchanged groups by role+message reference vector), reducing unnecessary MessageGroupCard rerenders; (2) moved render path to precomputed  instead of rebuilding grouping logic inline each render; (3) hardened scroll owner by disabling browser scroll anchoring on chat timeline container () to avoid tug-of-war with manual bottom-follow logic. Validated via frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch/dedup) and lint.

title: Remove live padding and enforce strict authoritative convergence to eliminate tail user duplicates
description:
Requested UX correction + data correctness fix:
1) Remove live inner padding introduced for streaming.
2) Fix duplicate user message appearing at end of chat.

Root cause hypothesis
- Authoritative merge currently preserves unmatched optimistic local messages, which can leave a tmp user message appended at tail when server timeline already has the persisted turn.

Deliverables
- Remove live padding wiring from ChatView/MessageGroupCard.
- Change authoritative merge contract to strict server convergence (no unmatched local preservation in authoritative mode).
- Update tests to assert authoritative convergence behavior.

Validation
- frontend tests (dedup/response-command-dispatch/trace-replay)
- frontend lint
close_reason: Removed streaming live padding from chat UI and fixed duplicate tail user message by enforcing strict authoritative convergence. ChatView no longer passes/applies liveInnerPadding in MessageGroupCard. mergeServerMessages(authoritative) now returns server timeline only (no unmatched optimistic local preservation), preventing tmp user duplicates at chat end. Updated dedup regression tests to assert authoritative convergence behavior. Validated with frontend tests (dedup/response-command-dispatch/trace-replay) and lint.

title: Streaming line preallocation inside live markdown block to reduce expansion lag
description:
UX polish: during live markdown streaming, preallocate vertical room inside the assistant content block so container expansion leads line reveal instead of trailing it.

Deliverables
- add deterministic in-block min-height reservation tied to current streamed line count + one lookahead line
- keep behavior scoped to streaming state only
- no outer container gaps

Validation
- frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Implemented in-block streaming line preallocation while keeping live markdown: TextWithFileReferences now reserves deterministic min-height during streaming based on current line count + one lookahead line, with a short min-height transition. This lets container expansion lead line reveal without introducing outer gaps. Validated via frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch) and lint.

title: Improve streaming block preallocation for wrapped lines (thinking + text)
description:
Current in-block streaming min-height uses newline count and still lags for wrapped content, especially thinking blocks and long prose lines.

Deliverables
- adjust reservation model to include wrapped-line estimation from character volume, not only explicit newlines
- keep behavior scoped to streaming mode
- preserve live markdown rendering

Validation
- frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Improved streaming in-block preallocation to account for wrapped text, not just explicit newlines. TextWithFileReferences now computes reserved lines as max(logical lines, char-volume wrapped-line estimate) plus lookahead, reducing expansion lag in thinking and normal response blocks while preserving live markdown. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch) and lint.

title: Frontend: property-style invariant tests for mergeServerMessages + normalizeMessages
description:
Problem
Message merge behavior still regresses when uncommon payload combinations occur.

Deliverables

  - id/client_id reconciliation

Added tool replay preservation test and malformed/legacy normalization behavior checks. Added regression fixture seed at frontend/tests/fixtures/message-property-seeds/2026-04-14-finalized-tmp-assistant-drop.json. Also removed dead cruft in the interleaving harness (unused FetchOp.name) in frontend/tests/message-interleaving-regression.test.ts. Validation: bun run test -- message-merge-properties; bun run test (26 files, 138 tests).

title: Root-cause fix for streaming overflow/twitch: remove animated preallocation and unify staged reveal
description:
Implement root-cause corrective architecture for streaming jitter/overflow:
1) remove animated min-height preallocation (instant reserve only),
2) apply same staged reveal + reservation model to thinking and normal text,
3) keep message card clipped during streaming (no overflow-visible escape path),
4) two-phase streaming reveal: reserve first, reveal next frame.

Validation: frontend tests + lint.
close_reason: Implemented root-cause streaming jitter/overflow fix: removed animated min-height preallocation (instant reserve only), introduced shared two-phase staged streaming reveal hook (reserve frame then reveal frame), unified reservation estimator usage across text and thinking render paths, and removed assistant overflow-visible escape in MessageGroupCard so streaming content stays clipped to card bounds. Preserved live markdown. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch/dedup) and lint.

title: Fix streaming bottom bleed-through and keep wrap distribution identical after persist
description:
User-reported regressions after root-cause pass:
1) bottom lines still appear outside message container while streaming,
2) word/line distribution differs between streaming and persisted states.

Plan
- eliminate streaming-only wrap policy divergence (same wrap policy during and after streaming),
- remove streaming-only trailing-word buffering that changes final line breaks,
- tighten clipping/containment around text and thinking markdown surfaces during live updates.

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Addressed streaming/persist visual divergence and bleed-through: removed trailing-word buffering in live text/thinking path so streamed and final content use same token stream; enforced identical wrap policy () during streaming and after persist to prevent post-persist reflow shifts; added paint/layout containment + overflow-hiding hardening on live text and thinking markdown wrappers to keep bottom lines clipped inside message container while streaming. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch/dedup) and lint.

title: Stabilize bottom viewport edge during live streaming auto-follow
description:
User reports bottom container line still twitches while streaming expansion. Goal: when user is at bottom and not manually scrolling, keep bottom edge visually fixed (strict bottom lock) during stream updates.

Deliverables
- inspect/adjust auto-follow scroll loop
- enforce stable bottom lock while auto-follow active
- preserve behavior when user scrolls up (no forced snap)

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Implemented strict bottom-lock behavior for live streaming auto-follow. ChatView now computes explicit bottom target (scrollHeight - clientHeight) for bottom writes and adds a layout-phase bottom-lock effect while stream/await/send is active and user has not scrolled up. This keeps the bottom edge visually fixed and prevents twitch at the container boundary while preserving normal behavior when user scrolls away. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch/dedup) and lint.

title: Inner assistant message-container bottom lock during streaming
description:
Extend bottom-edge stabilization to inner message container: when auto-follow is active and assistant group is live, keep inner content block bottom visually fixed while container reserves/grows.

Deliverables

- reserve min-height at group level for live segments
- no effect when user scrolled up or non-live groups

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Superseded by oqto-zpvs.24 implementation, which addressed the reported full-message twitch and inner visual instability via direct stream rendering + line-break stabilization.

title: Reduce full-message streaming text twitch (remove staged reveal, stabilize line breaking)
description:
User reports the complete message text still twitches while streaming.

Plan
- remove staged two-frame text reveal path and render direct stream text to avoid bursty whole-block updates,
- disable hyphenation-driven reflow in markdown content so existing lines don't reshuffle aggressively as content grows,
- keep bottom-lock behavior.

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Reduced full-message text twitch during streaming: removed staged two-frame reveal path so text updates render directly (no bursty whole-block swaps), and disabled hyphenation-driven reflow in markdown () so lines do not reshuffle aggressively as tokens append. Kept existing bottom-lock behavior. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch/dedup) and lint.

title: Fix duplicated tool-call rows during streaming before persistence convergence
description:
Regression: during live streaming, tool calls/results can render duplicated in assistant message body; once persisted timeline arrives duplicates disappear. Suspected cause is normalization path that attaches a tool_result to tool_call while also leaving adjacent tool_result_only segment renderable.

Deliverables
- update MessageGroupCard segment normalization to consume/suppress duplicate adjacent tool_result_only when already attached to tool_call
- preserve correct standalone tool_result rendering when no matching call exists

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Fixed live tool duplication in MessageGroupCard normalization: when a tool_call already has an attached result (matched by id or name), adjacent duplicate tool_result_only segments are now consumed instead of rendered again. Also keeps prior behavior of pairing standalone next tool_result_only when no attached result exists. This removes streaming-time duplicate tool rows while preserving standalone orphan result rendering. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch/dedup/message-interleaving-regression) and lint.

title: Resize-aware tail-commit streaming with smooth line slide-in
description:
Implement streaming UX model requested by user: hold back incomplete tail during streaming, commit on boundary, animate committed chunk sliding in from bottom, and handle viewport resize safely.

Deliverables
- streaming text/tail split in message renderer (committed + hidden tail),
- boundary-based commit policy,
- smooth slide-in for newly committed chunk,
- resize epoch that temporarily disables animation while preserving bottom lock.

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Implemented resize-aware tail-commit streaming UX: added committed-tail split during streaming (holds back incomplete tail until boundary), smooth slide-in animation for newly committed chunks, and resize-settle gating that temporarily suppresses commit animation during viewport resize to avoid reflow jitter. Applied to both normal text and thinking markdown paths while preserving existing bottom-lock behavior. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch/dedup/message-interleaving-regression) and lint.

title: Smooth boundary commit animation retrigger + freeze live updates while user scrolled up
description:
Follow-up regression after oqto-zpvs.26: (1) commit chunks appear abruptly because animation does not reliably retrigger on consecutive commits, (2) while user scrolls up, live incoming updates still cause subtle twitch in visible viewport.

Deliverables
- retrigger commit animation every boundary commit via alternating animation classes/tokens,
- freeze streaming text + reservation basis while user is scrolled up,
- preserve current bottom-lock behavior when user is at bottom.

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Addressed follow-up streaming UX regressions: (1) commit animation now retriggers on every boundary commit via alternating animation phase classes (A/B), eliminating abrupt non-animated inserts on consecutive commits; (2) added freezeStreamingUpdates path so live streaming text and reservation basis are frozen while user is scrolled up, preventing viewport twitch while reading historical position. Applied to both normal text and thinking markdown flows, while preserving bottom-lock behavior when at bottom. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch/dedup/message-interleaving-regression) and lint.

Chat renderer now switches by mode: chunked mode retains boundary-commit behavior with smaller tail chunks (max tail 10 chars) for finer cadence; smooth mode streams continuously and applies per-delta crawl tick animation. Existing freeze-while-scrolled-up and bottom-lock behaviors remain intact. Added shared streaming-presentation store module and wired mode through ChatView -> MessageGroupCard -> part/text renderers. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch/dedup/message-interleaving-regression) and lint.

Streaming presentation store accepts and persists raw mode. ChatView renderer paths are mode-aware: chunked uses smaller holdback chunks (max tail 6 chars), smooth uses crawl tick animation with no chunk holdback, raw bypasses chunk holdback and disables commit animation for direct token-level rendering. Existing freeze-while-scrolled-up and bottom-lock behavior preserved. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/response-command-dispatch/dedup/message-interleaving-regression) and lint.

title: Make streaming presentation modes behaviorally distinct (chunked/smooth/raw)
description:
User feedback: new 3-mode selector appears visually identical; all modes feel token-level.

Root cause hypothesis
- chunked split policy currently releases almost every token (tail too small + boundary at last whitespace),
- smooth crawl tick delta too subtle to distinguish from raw.

Deliverables
- enforce true chunked gating: commit only at boundaries with minimum pending chunk size
- increase smooth visual distinction from raw while preserving readability
- keep raw as direct token-level path without animation

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Made streaming modes clearly distinct. Chunked mode now uses true boundary gating with minimum pending tail (24 chars) so updates arrive in readable chunks instead of near-token cadence. Smooth mode now uses real push-up transform animation (height-delta based) for continuous sliding crawl feel on each live update. Raw mode remains direct token-level rendering with no smoothing animation. Kept freeze-while-scrolled-up and bottom-lock behavior. Validated with frontend tests (response-command-dispatch/dedup) and lint.

title: Smooth crawl: eliminate repeated downward twitch during rapid streaming deltas
description:
User reports smooth mode still twitches downward constantly. Likely cause: each delta resets transform from zero and restarts transition, producing visible downward snaps.

Deliverables
- in smooth transform hook, accumulate from current transform offset and restart animation from accumulated value
- gate tiny deltas to avoid micro jitter
- keep smooth push-up effect and bottom lock

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Refined smooth crawl transform to eliminate repeated downward twitch during rapid streaming deltas: now accumulates from current translateY offset, ignores tiny height deltas (<=1.5px), and restarts easing from accumulated offset instead of snapping from zero each update. Also cancels pending RAF on mode change/unmount and clears inline styles safely. Preserves smooth push-up behavior and existing bottom-lock/freeze logic. Validated with frontend lint and targeted tests (response-command-dispatch/dedup).

title: Smooth mode: switch to fully continuous crawl motion (no discrete stepping)
description:
User reports smooth mode still exhibits discrete jumps; requirement is continuous smooth slide-in only.

Deliverables
- replace per-delta transition restart with RAF-driven continuous offset model (additive incoming delta + gradual decay to zero)
- avoid any transform reset on each delta
- keep shared behavior for normal text and thinking blocks

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Implemented fully continuous smooth crawl motion (no discrete step transitions): replaced per-delta transition resets with RAF-driven additive offset model (incoming height deltas accumulate into offset; offset decays continuously each frame to zero). Added micro-delta gate and robust RAF/style cleanup. This yields continuous slide-in/push-up behavior instead of hard jump ticks. Shared hook is used by both normal text and thinking blocks, so parity is preserved. Validated with frontend tests (response-command-dispatch/dedup) and lint.

title: Smooth mode: remove container twitch by disabling per-delta reservation growth
description:
User reports text crawl is now smooth but message container still twitches. Likely cause is per-delta minHeight reservation growth in streaming wrappers.

Deliverables
- make reservation/minHeight behavior chunked-mode only
- in smooth/raw, rely on natural content height + smooth internal crawl transform
- apply same gating to thinking wrappers

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Removed container twitch in smooth/raw by making streaming min-height reservation chunked-only. Text and thinking wrappers now apply reservation/contain styles only when mode=chunked; smooth/raw rely on natural content height plus smooth crawl transform. This eliminates per-delta reservation growth jitter on message container while preserving chunked readability behavior and parity for thinking blocks. Validated with frontend tests (response-command-dispatch/dedup) and lint.

title: Smooth mode lockstep: animate container growth with crawl text (no discrete jumps)
description:
User requirement: in smooth mode the message container must move in lockstep with text crawl; no discrete bubble jumps.

Deliverables

- keep text crawl transform + container height animation synchronized
- apply to normal text and thinking blocks

Validation: frontend tests + lint
close_reason: Implemented lockstep smooth-mode container motion with continuous RAF height smoothing. Added useSmoothContainerHeight hook that animates wrapper height toward live measured content height every frame (no discrete minHeight jumps) and wired it alongside smooth crawl transform for both normal text and thinking blocks. Smooth mode now moves container and text together; chunked/raw behavior unchanged. Validated with frontend tests (response-command-dispatch/dedup) and lint.

title: Fix missing intermediate user message in grouped chat rendering

This preserves legitimate intermediate user turns while still dropping truly empty steer echoes. Validated with frontend tests (trace-replay/message-interleaving-regression/dedup/response-command-dispatch) and lint.

title: Remove partial-snapshot blanket user-role drop causing hidden user turns
description: Investigate missing intermediate user messages: useChat applyServerMessages currently drops all user/human rows for partial snapshots. Replace with deterministic merge path that does not blanket-remove user turns.
close_reason: Confirmed root cause heuristic: partial applyServerMessages dropped all user/human rows, which can hide legitimate repeated verbatim user turns until/if authoritative sync catches up. Replaced blanket drop with deterministic client_id correlation: in partial mode, user rows are accepted only when their client_id matches a locally tracked optimistic user message. This preserves real repeated sends while still blocking stale cross-session user contamination (trace-replay session-switch invariant remains green). Validated via frontend tests (trace-replay/message-interleaving-regression/dedup/response-command-dispatch/use-chat-send-reliability) and lint.

title: Fix streaming tool duplication and message loss in near-ngos-spin session
description: User reports in session 'Solving Ssh Forwarding Permissions for Oqto Agents' (readable_id near-ngos-spin, 2026/04/19) that tool calls still duplicate during streaming and messages are still lost. Reproduce exact session, compare API vs UI timeline, and fix deterministic merge/render contracts.

title: Backend: integration tests for persist-before-idle and live-vs-authoritative fetch contracts
description:
Problem
Critical ordering and source-of-truth contracts are not guarded by integration tests.

Deliverables

  1) persist to store completes before agent.idle broadcast
  2) active-session message retrieval semantics (partial/live window behavior)
  3) inactive-session retrieval semantics (authoritative persisted history)

Validation

Added persistence order contract tests for idle-vs-persist sequencing. Validated with TMPDIR=/home/wismut/byteowlz/oqto_refactor/.tmp cargo test -p oqto persistence_contracts.

title: Fix streaming message loss when switching sessions and returning
description: User reports in-flight streaming messages disappear after switching to another session and back. Reproduce with active stream, inspect useChat session-switch snapshot/cache path, and ensure deterministic preservation without stale cross-session contamination.
close_reason: Fixed switch-away/switch-back streaming loss in frontend: fetchHistoryMessages now supports preserveInFlightCache and only downgrades to partial merge when an in-flight local/cached assistant tmp snapshot exists during session re-entry. This prevents authoritative history fetch from clobbering active streamed content on return, while settled turns still converge authoritatively. Verified with trace-replay + reliability regression tests and manual session switch during active stream.

title: Session timeline contract: strict per-session live buffer recovery + authoritative durable fetch split
description:
Problem
We still observe message disappearance/reordering and cross-session contamination risk during parallel streaming + rapid session switching. Root cause class is contract ambiguity between live buffer and durable history retrieval.

Current failure mode
- Frontend treats idle/reconnect fetch path as authoritative timeline reconciliation.
- Backend may return live-buffer snapshots on active sessions from the same API path.
- Live snapshots can be partial/position-ID based and are unsuitable for destructive authoritative merges.
- Result: dropped older turns, unstable ordering, and regressions that reappear under interleavings.

Goal (hard invariants)
1) Live streaming remains fully enabled for parallel sessions.
2) Runner live buffer is strictly per-session.
3) Session switch/reconnect/reload never causes message loss.
4) Cross-session contamination is impossible by construction.
5) Authoritative timeline fetch is always durable and deterministic.

Required architecture contract
A. Split read semantics explicitly:
- Durable path (authoritative): returns only persisted timeline (oqto-log/hstry during migration), ordered append-only.
- Live path (volatile): returns per-session runner buffer snapshot, explicitly marked non-authoritative.

B. Session isolation:
- Every live/durable read/write keyed by session id.
- No global active-session fallback in backend or frontend reducers.
- Stale async results dropped by session id + epoch/version guard.

C. Reconciliation rules:
- stream events + live snapshot => partial merge only.
- idle/reload/reconnect durable fetch => authoritative merge only.
- never run authoritative merge on live snapshot payloads.

Deliverables
1) Backend API/runner contract
- Introduce explicit source separation for message retrieval:
  - get_chat_messages_authoritative(session_id)
  - get_chat_messages_live(session_id) (or equivalent command/flag with explicit source metadata)
- Ensure existing chat history endpoint used by frontend authoritative reconcile never returns live buffer.
- Include source metadata + version/sequence where applicable.

2) Frontend enforcement
- Enforce merge mode by source:
  - durable source => authoritative
  - live source => partial
- Guard all async applies with session_id and transport epoch/version checks.

3) Identity stability
- Ensure per-session live snapshot identity is stable enough for reconciliation (avoid positional-only IDs leaking into authoritative path).

4) Regression net
- Add deterministic tests for:
  - parallel active streams A/B with rapid switching
  - reconnect mid-stream per session
  - reload mid-stream
  - stale live response arriving after durable response
  - no lost messages, no reorder drift, no cross-session contamination after settle

Acceptance criteria
- With two parallel streaming sessions, switching tabs/sessions rapidly does not lose or reorder messages.
- Durable fetch path yields identical timeline before/after reload/reconnect once settled.
- Live fetch path can recover dropped frontend stream deltas without destructive clobber.
- All new tests pass and fail under intentional contract violation.

Validation commands
- cd backend && cargo test -p oqto session_identity_isolation persistence_contracts
- cd frontend && bun run test -- message-interleaving-regression trace-replay
- Add dedicated new suite command(s) for parallel-stream no-loss scenarios.

Out of scope
- Any backwards compatibility shim that keeps ambiguous mixed-source behavior alive.
close_reason: Implemented strict source split for session message retrieval: runner protocol now includes WorkspaceChatMessagesSource (authoritative/live) on request+response; REST chat history path now explicitly requests authoritative source only; WS get_messages now explicitly requests live source and emits messages_source metadata for frontend merge-mode enforcement. Runner daemon contracts now separate authoritative and live selectors (no mixed-source fallback). Frontend useChat enforces durable fetches as authoritative, live get_messages as partial, and adds transportEpoch stale-result guard in history fetch path for reconnect races. Added regression coverage in frontend response-command-dispatch tests for authoritative vs live merge modes. Validated with backend persistence/session isolation test suites and frontend trace replay/interleaving/dispatch tests plus frontend lint.

title: Eliminate heuristic text-based dedup that drops optimistic user turns
description:
Problem
Authoritative merge currently uses user-text equality as a fallback dedup signal when clientId is missing. This can drop legitimate optimistic user turns (especially repeated short prompts like 'ok'/'yes') before persistence confirms them.

Required fix
- Remove text-based dedup heuristics from authoritative merge.
- Preserve unmatched optimistic user turns unless reconciled by stable identity (clientId/id) or explicit authoritative supersession contract.
- Do not infer duplication from same text content alone.

Deliverables
1) frontend message merge cleanup
- remove serverUserTexts/extractUserMessageText fallback in mergeServerMessages authoritative branch
- keep deterministic id/clientId reconciliation only

2) regression tests
- repeated identical user text across turns must never disappear during authoritative fetch
- optimistic unmatched user turn remains until server echoes matching clientId

Validation
- cd frontend && bun run test -- dedup response-command-dispatch trace-replay
- cd frontend && bun run lint

Unmatched optimistic user turns are now preserved unless reconciled by stable identity (clientId/id). Added regression test covering repeated identical user text so optimistic message is not dropped before persistence echo. Validated with frontend tests (dedup/response-command-dispatch/trace-replay) and frontend lint.

description:
Integrate byt (byteowlz meta-tool) for project scaffolding within octo workspaces.

## What is byt?
byt is the byteowlz meta-tool for cross-repo management, including project scaffolding from templates.

## Features

### 1. Project Scaffolding
```bash
byt new myproject --template rust-cli
byt new myproject --template python-cli
byt new myproject --template go-cli
```

### 2. Available Templates
- rust-cli: Single-binary Rust CLI
- rust-workspace: Multi-crate Rust workspace
- python-cli: Python CLI with uv
- go-cli: Go CLI

### 3. Agent Integration
- Agent can scaffold new projects
- Creates proper structure with AGENTS.md
- Initializes git, adds standard files

## Implementation

### Container
- Add byt binary to container image
- Configure templates path or remote fetch
- Add scaffolding skill

### Skill Definition
```yaml
---
name: scaffolding
description: Create new projects from templates using byt
---
Use byt to scaffold new projects:
- byt new <name> --template rust-cli
- byt new <name> --template python-cli

Templates include proper project structure, AGENTS.md, and tooling config.
```

### Templates Location
Options:
1. Bundle templates in container image
2. Fetch from byteowlz/templates repo
3. User-provided template directory

## Dependencies
- byt binary available
- Template repository access

title: Orchestrator: Handle orphan containers and port conflicts
description:
The session orchestrator now handles two critical multi-user scenarios:

## 1. Orphan Container Cleanup

**Problem:** When the backend crashes or restarts, containers may be left running without corresponding database sessions. These 'orphan' containers hold ports and prevent new sessions from starting.

**Solution:** Added startup cleanup that:
- Detects containers with 'opencode-' prefix that have no matching database session
- Safely removes orphan containers at server startup
- Marks stale database sessions (whose containers no longer exist) as failed

**Key methods in SessionService:**
- `startup_cleanup()` - runs once at server start
- `find_orphan_containers()` - identifies orphans by comparing running containers to DB
- `cleanup_orphan_containers()` - stops and removes orphan containers
- `cleanup_stale_sessions()` - marks DB sessions as failed if container is gone

## 2. Port Allocation Race Condition

**Problem:** When multiple users create sessions simultaneously:
1. Both call `find_free_port_range()` and get the same ports
2. Both try to create containers with those ports  
3. One succeeds, one fails with port conflict

**Solution:** Two-layer protection:

### Layer 1: Database Unique Constraints (migration 20241217001)
```sql
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_sessions_active_opencode_port 
ON sessions(opencode_port) 
WHERE status IN ('pending', 'starting', 'running');
```

### Layer 2: Retry Logic in Service
- `create_session()` now wraps `try_create_session()` in a retry loop
- On unique constraint violation, retries with different port range
- Max 5 retries with exponential backoff
- `is_port_conflict_error()` detects constraint violations

## Files Changed
- `backend/src/session/service.rs` - cleanup methods and retry logic
- `backend/src/main.rs` - calls `startup_cleanup()` on server start
- `backend/migrations/20241217001_port_allocation.sql` - unique indexes on ports

2. ghostty-web init() may be slow (loading WASM)
3. authToken not available immediately (login happens async in AppContext)

## Investigation Needed
- Add timing logs to identify which step is slow
- Check if WASM init is cached properly
- Check when authToken becomes available vs when effect runs

User modes: Simplified, Light Coding, Terminal Terminator
description:
Three distinct user experience levels that control UI visibility and features.

User Modes:

1. SIMPLIFIED (PowerPoint Juggler)
   - File browser: Flat list, office files only (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf)
   - Terminal: Hidden, not connected
   - Chat: Full access
   - Model switcher: Hidden (use default model)
   - Agents: Only 'main' agent visible

2. LIGHT CODING (Engineer)  
   - File browser: Full tree view, all files
   - Terminal: Hidden by default, can be enabled
   - Chat: Full access
   - Model switcher: VISIBLE (dropdown in chat header)
   - Agents: All agents visible

3. TERMINAL TERMINATOR (CLI-Junky)
   - File browser: Full tree view, all files
   - Terminal: Visible and prominent
   - Chat: Full access
   - Model switcher: VISIBLE (dropdown in chat header)
   - Agents: All agents visible, can create new

Implementation:

1. Store user_mode in user profile (backend)

2. Frontend mode context

   - Mode-aware component rendering

3. First login mode selection (workspace-p54)

   - Three cards explaining each mode
   - Selected mode saved to profile

4. Mode switcher in settings
   - Allow changing mode anytime
   - Settings page or user menu dropdown

5. Component adaptations:
   - FileBrowser: respect mode for view/filter

   - ModelSwitcher: show for modes 2 & 3

Add WebSocket support for real-time events
description:
Add WebSocket support to the backend for real-time bidirectional communication.

## Why WebSocket?

Current SSE (Server-Sent Events) is one-way. We need bidirectional for:
1. **Permission requests** - Agent asks, user responds
2. **Live voice mode** - Continuous audio streaming both ways
3. **Interrupt/cancel** - User can stop agent mid-execution
4. **Real-time presence** - Multiple users/agents coordination

## Implementation

### Backend (Rust/Axum)
```rust
// New WebSocket endpoint
GET /ws/session/:id -> WebSocket upgrade

// Message types
enum WsMessage {
    // Agent -> User
    PermissionRequest { id, tool, args, context },
    AgentOutput { content, done },
    StatusUpdate { status },
    
    // User -> Agent  
    PermissionResponse { id, allowed },
    UserMessage { content },
    Cancel { },
    
    // Both ways
    Ping, Pong
}
```

### Frontend
- Add WebSocket client (native or socket.io-client)
- Reconnection logic with exponential backoff
- Message queue for offline/reconnecting state
- Hook into existing event handling

### Proxy Considerations
- Backend proxies WS to container's opencode
- Or: opencode exposes WS, backend just routes
- Check opencode's WS capabilities

## Dependencies
- May unblock: Live voice mode, Permission display fix

title: Scheduled tasks / Cron system
description:
Add scheduled task execution for automated agent runs.

## Features

### 1. Task Scheduling
- Cron-style scheduling (e.g., '0 9 * * *' for daily 9am)
- One-time scheduled runs (run at specific datetime)
- Recurring intervals (every 30m, every 2h)

### 2. Task Types
- **Agent runs**: Execute a prompt at scheduled time
- **Maintenance**: Backup workspace, cleanup artifacts
- **Notifications**: Daily summaries, reminders
- **Webhooks**: Trigger external services

### 3. Task Definition
```toml
[[tasks]]
id = "daily-standup"
schedule = "0 9 * * 1-5"  # 9am weekdays
persona = "govnr"
prompt = "Review my calendar and prepare standup notes"
deliver_to = "session"  # or email, webhook

[[tasks]]
id = "weekly-cleanup"
schedule = "0 0 * * 0"  # Sunday midnight
action = "cleanup"
options = { older_than_days = 7 }
```

### 4. UI Components
- Task list with next run time
- Create/edit task form
- Run history with logs
- Enable/disable toggle
- Manual trigger button

## Implementation

### Backend
```rust
// New module: backend/src/scheduler/
struct ScheduledTask {
    id: String,
    user_id: String,
    schedule: CronSchedule,
    task_type: TaskType,
    config: TaskConfig,
    enabled: bool,
    last_run: Option<DateTime>,
    next_run: DateTime,
}

enum TaskType {
    AgentRun { persona_id: String, prompt: String },
    Maintenance { action: String },
    Webhook { url: String, payload: Value },
}
```

### Database
```sql
CREATE TABLE scheduled_tasks (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    schedule TEXT NOT NULL,
    task_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    config TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON
    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    last_run_at TEXT,
    next_run_at TEXT,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE TABLE task_runs (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    task_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    completed_at TEXT,
    status TEXT NOT NULL,  -- running, success, failed
    output TEXT,
    error TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (task_id) REFERENCES scheduled_tasks(id)
);
```

### Scheduler Service
- Background tokio task checking next_run times
- Spawn agent sessions for AgentRun tasks
- Execute maintenance actions
- Record run history

### API Endpoints
```
GET    /api/tasks              # List user's tasks
POST   /api/tasks              # Create task
GET    /api/tasks/:id          # Get task details
PUT    /api/tasks/:id          # Update task
DELETE /api/tasks/:id          # Delete task
POST   /api/tasks/:id/run      # Trigger immediate run
GET    /api/tasks/:id/runs     # Get run history
```

## Cron Library
Use `cron` or `tokio-cron-scheduler` crate for parsing and scheduling.

title: Integrate A2UI (Artifact-to-UI) support
description:
Add A2UI support for rendering agent-generated artifacts in a visual canvas.

## What is A2UI?
A2UI (Artifact-to-UI) is a protocol for agents to generate visual artifacts (HTML/CSS/JS) that render in a dedicated canvas/iframe.

## Features

### 1. A2UI Canvas Component
- Sandboxed iframe for rendering agent artifacts
- PostMessage bridge for communication
- Live reload on artifact updates

### 2. Artifact Types
- HTML pages (static or interactive)
- Charts/visualizations (D3, Chart.js)
- Diagrams (Mermaid, Excalidraw)
- Forms and UI prototypes
- Markdown previews

### 3. Agent Integration
- Agent writes to designated artifacts directory
- Frontend watches for changes
- Auto-renders new/updated artifacts

### 4. UI Layout
- Split view: Chat + Artifact canvas
- Artifact selector (when multiple)
- Full-screen artifact mode
- Download/export artifacts

## Implementation

### Backend
- Serve artifacts directory via fileserver
- WebSocket notifications for artifact changes

### Frontend
- A2UICanvas component with iframe
- Artifact file watcher (via fileserver /ws/watch)
- PostMessage protocol handler

### Container
- Artifacts directory: `~/workspace/.artifacts/`
- Agent writes HTML/assets here
- Fileserver serves with correct MIME types

## Reference
- Clawdis Canvas implementation
- Claude Artifacts

title: Dictation should work while focused in input box
description: When dictation mode is active and user clicks inside the text input box to position cursor or type manually, the dictation stops filling in transcribed words. Expected behavior: dictation should continue appending words to the input regardless of whether the input is focused or not. User should be able to: 1) Have dictation running, 2) Click into the input box, 3) Type manually with keyboard, 4) And dictation still appends transcribed speech to the input. The two modes should work together seamlessly.

title: Integrate mmry for agent memory
description:
Integrate mmry (byteowlz memory system) for persistent agent memory across sessions.

## What is mmry?
mmry is a local-first memory system for humans and AI agents. It provides semantic search, categorization, and cross-session memory persistence.

## Features

### 1. Per-User Memory Store
- Each user gets a dedicated mmry store
- Memory persists across sessions
- Agent can add/search memories

### 2. Agent Integration
- Install mmry in container
- Add memory skill for agent usage
- Auto-context injection on session start

### 3. Memory Operations
```bash
mmry add "learned insight" -c category -t tag1,tag2
mmry search "query" --limit 10
mmry ls --limit 20
```

### 4. Session Context
On session start:
- Search relevant memories for current task
- Inject as context in AGENTS.md or system prompt

## Implementation

### Container
- Add mmry binary to container image
- Configure store path: `~/.local/share/mmry/`
- Add memory skill: `.opencode/skill/memory/SKILL.md`

### Backend
- Mount mmry data directory
- Persist across container restarts

### Skill Definition
```yaml
---
name: memory
description: Store and retrieve persistent memories across sessions
---
Use mmry to remember important information:
- mmry add "insight" -c category
- mmry search "query"
- mmry ls --limit 10

Categories: decisions, preferences, learnings, context
```

Fix React Strict Mode double-invoke breaking WebSocket connections in GhosttyTerminal
description:
## Problem
The GhosttyTerminal React component fails to establish WebSocket connections reliably due to React 18 Strict Mode double-invoking effects.

## Symptoms
- Console shows 'Terminal: connecting...' appearing 2-3 times
- Firefox error: 'can't establish a connection to the server'
- Error: 'connection was interrupted while the page was loading'
- Terminal stuck on 'Connecting...' status

## Root Cause
React Strict Mode (development only) intentionally double-invokes:
1. Effect runs, creates WebSocket (state: CONNECTING)
2. Cleanup runs immediately, closes socket
3. Effect runs again, creates new WebSocket
4. Both sockets interfere or the cleanup timing corrupts state

## What We've Tried (All Failed)
1. `setupDoneRef` to skip second invocation - doesn't work because cleanup resets it
2. `connectingRef` to track connection state - race condition with async
3. Checking `socketRef.current.readyState` - socket is CONNECTING not OPEN
4. Separating terminal init and WebSocket into different effects - still double-invokes
5. Single combined effect with `cancelled` flag - cleanup still interrupts connection

## Evidence WebSocket Works
```bash
# This works perfectly outside React:
bun run test-ws.ts  # -> Connected! Message received...
```

## Files Involved
- frontend/components/terminal/ghostty-terminal.tsx
- frontend/components/app-context.tsx (provides authToken)
- backend/src/api/proxy.rs (WebSocket proxy to ttyd)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Terminal connects successfully on page load
- [ ] Only one WebSocket connection is created
- [ ] Works in both development (Strict Mode) and production
- [ ] Reconnects properly when authToken changes

mmry proxy in single-user mode doesn't pass store parameter - all sessions show same memories
description:
## Problem

In single-user mode, the mmry proxy routes all session requests to the same local mmry service URL without specifying which store to use. This causes all sessions (octo, govnr, etc.) to display the same memories from the default store.

## Root Cause

In `backend/src/api/proxy.rs`, the `proxy_mmry_list` and related handlers call:
```rust
let target_url = get_mmry_target(&state, &session)?;
proxy_request_to_url(..., "v1/memories").await
```

The request goes to `v1/memories` without a `?store=<name>` query parameter. mmry defaults to its default store when no store is specified.

## Solution

1. Derive store name from session's workspace_path (e.g., `/home/user/byteowlz/octo` -> `octo`)
2. Append `?store=<name>` to all proxied mmry requests in single-user mode
3. Merge with existing query parameters if present

## Affected Endpoints

- GET /session/{id}/memories (list)
- POST /session/{id}/memories (add)  
- POST /session/{id}/memories/search
- GET/PUT/DELETE /session/{id}/memories/{memory_id}
- GET /session/{id}/memories/stores

title: Context Window Gauge: Dynamic model limits from models.dev
description:
Implemented dynamic context window limits for the ContextWindowGauge component by integrating with models.dev API.

## Changes

### New Hook: `/frontend/hooks/use-models-dev.ts`
- Fetches model data from `https://models.dev/api.json`
- Cached for 1 hour with TanStack Query (staleTime: 1hr, gcTime: 24hr)
- Exports:
  - `useModelsDevData()` - fetches all model data
  - `useModelContextLimit(providerID, modelID, defaultLimit)` - returns context limit for specific model
  - `getContextLimit()` - helper function for direct lookups

### Updated: `/frontend/apps/sessions/index.tsx`
- `tokenUsage` memo now extracts `providerID` and `modelID` from most recent assistant message
- Added `contextLimit` using `useModelContextLimit()` hook
- Both desktop and mobile ContextWindowGauge instances use dynamic `maxTokens={contextLimit}`

## How It Works
1. Messages loaded -> extract providerID/modelID from latest assistant message
2. Hook fetches models.dev data (cached) -> looks up `providers[providerID].models[modelID].limit.context`
3. Returns actual context limit or falls back to 200,000 tokens

## Example Context Limits
- Claude Opus/Sonnet: 200,000 tokens
- DeepSeek V3: 163,840 tokens
- Qwen Max: 32,768 tokens
- Gemini Pro: 2,097,152 tokens (2M)

title: Persona system: Core architecture
description:
Implement persona selection system that lets users choose a persona for each chat session.

A **Persona** combines four components:
1. **Workdir** - Working directory with AGENTS.md (job description)
2. **Permissions** - opencode built-in tool permissions configuration
3. **Tools** - Available MCP tools and external integrations  
4. **Skills** - Skills at `.opencode/skill/<name>/SKILL.md` (opencode discovery path)

## Implementation Approach: TBD

**Option 1: x-opencode-directory header (simpler if it works)**
- Single opencode server per container
- Pass directory per request via `x-opencode-directory` header
- **BLOCKER**: Need to verify session behavior - see workspace-npk

**Option 2: One opencode server per persona (current multi-agent approach)**
- Multiple opencode instances on different ports
- Each persona = separate opencode server in its directory
- Already partially implemented in workspace-ivc

**Decision**: Investigate x-opencode-directory first (workspace-npk), fall back to Option 2 if it doesn't work cleanly.

## Data Model

```
Persona {
  id: string
  name: string
  description: string
  workdir: string           // path to persona directory
  permissions: {
    edit: 'allow' | 'ask' | 'deny'
    bash: 'allow' | 'ask' | 'deny' | Record<string, 'allow'|'ask'|'deny'>
    skill: 'allow' | 'ask' | 'deny' | Record<string, 'allow'|'ask'|'deny'>
    webfetch: 'allow' | 'ask' | 'deny'
    doom_loop: 'allow' | 'ask' | 'deny'
    external_directory: 'allow' | 'ask' | 'deny'
  }
  tools: string[]           // enabled MCP tool names
  color: string             // UI accent color
  icon: string              // optional icon/emoji
}
```

## Persona Directory Structure

```
~/workspace/personas/
├── govnr/                  # Default general-purpose persona
│   ├── AGENTS.md           # Job description
│   ├── persona.toml        # Persona config (permissions, metadata)
│   └── .opencode/
│       ├── opencode.json   # Permissions config
│       └── skill/          # Persona-specific skills
│           └── general-coding/SKILL.md
└── doc-writer/
    ├── AGENTS.md
    ├── persona.toml
    └── .opencode/
        └── skill/
            └── technical-writing/SKILL.md
```

## Dependencies
- workspace-npk: Investigate x-opencode-directory behavior

## Components
- Backend: Persona registry, CRUD API, session binding
- Frontend: Persona picker UI, per-chat persona display
- Container: Depends on chosen approach

title: Persona system: Backend API
description:
Implement backend API for persona management.

## Endpoints

```
GET    /api/personas              # List all personas for user
GET    /api/personas/:id          # Get persona details
POST   /api/personas              # Create new persona
PUT    /api/personas/:id          # Update persona
DELETE /api/personas/:id          # Delete persona

POST   /api/sessions              # Add persona_id to create request
GET    /api/sessions/:id          # Include current persona in response
PATCH  /api/sessions/:id/persona  # Switch persona mid-session
```

## Database Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE personas (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  workdir TEXT NOT NULL,
  permissions TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON
  tools TEXT,                 -- JSON array
  skills TEXT,                -- JSON array  
  color TEXT,
  icon TEXT,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
);

-- Add persona_id to sessions
ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN persona_id TEXT REFERENCES personas(id);
```

## Implementation
- Add personas module to backend/src/
- Persona service with CRUD operations
- Scan workspace/personas/ for filesystem-based personas
- Merge DB personas with filesystem personas

title: Persona system: Container injection
description:
Inject persona configuration into opencode requests via header.

## Key Insight: No Container Changes Needed

opencode supports `x-opencode-directory` header - we just need to add this header when proxying requests.

## Implementation

### Backend Proxy Update

In `backend/src/api/proxy.rs`, add the directory header:

```rust
async fn proxy_to_opencode(
    session: &Session,
    persona: Option<&Persona>,
    req: Request,
) -> Result<Response> {
    let mut headers = req.headers().clone();
    
    // Add persona workdir if set
    if let Some(p) = persona {
        headers.insert(
            "x-opencode-directory",
            HeaderValue::from_str(&p.workdir)?
        );
    }
    
    // Forward to opencode...
}
```

### Persona Directory Setup

Each persona directory needs:
```
~/workspace/personas/<name>/
├── AGENTS.md              # Read by opencode automatically
├── persona.toml           # Our config (permissions, metadata)
└── .opencode/
    ├── opencode.json      # Permissions config
    └── skill/             # Persona-specific skills
        └── <skill-name>/SKILL.md
```

### opencode.json per Persona

Generate or maintain per-persona opencode.json:
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "permission": {
    "edit": "allow",
    "bash": "ask",
    "skill": "allow",
    "webfetch": "allow",
    "doom_loop": "ask",
    "external_directory": "ask"
  }
}
```

### What We DON'T Need
- ~~Multiple opencode servers~~
- ~~Container restart on persona switch~~
- ~~Environment variable injection~~
- ~~entrypoint.sh changes~~

## Benefits
- Instant persona switching (no restart)
- Simpler architecture
- Less resource usage
- Easier testing

container/skel/workspace/skills/

~/workspace/personas/<persona>/.opencode/skill/<name>/SKILL.md

Or use global skills at:

~/.config/opencode/skill/<name>/SKILL.md

title: Correctness: Race condition between port allocation query and container creation
Fix message flickering during polling updates
description:
Fixed assistant messages disappearing and reappearing when message parts were updated during agent work.

## Root Cause
1. loadMessages() was replacing the entire messages array without preserving object references
2. MessageGroupCard key was based on startIndex which changed when message structure changed
3. React was unmounting/remounting components unnecessarily

## Fix
- Added mergeMessages() function that preserves existing message object references when content hasn't changed
- Only returns new object reference when message has actually been modified (new parts, text changes, tool state changes)
- Changed MessageGroupCard key from `${group.role}-${group.startIndex}` to use first message ID (stable)
- Both loadMessages() and polling effect now use the same merge function
- Removed startTransition which could cause visual glitches

## Files Changed
- frontend/apps/sessions/index.tsx

Architecture: Support multiple opencode agents per container
description:
Each container runs multiple opencode serve instances. The MAIN agent runs in ~/workspace (the default). Additional agents live in ~/workspace/<agent_name>/ subdirectories, each with its own AGENTS.md and git context.

Architecture decisions:
- Filesystem is source of truth (no manifest files needed)
- Agent ID = directory name  
- Agent color derived from directory name hash
- Containers are long-lived (stop but don't remove)
- Control plane manages agents via docker exec + opencode API

How it works:
1. entrypoint.sh starts main agent on port 4096
2. Control plane starts sub-agents via: docker exec -d <container> bash -c 'cd ~/workspace/<agent> && opencode serve --port <port>'
3. Control plane checks agent status via opencode API (GET /path)
4. Control plane stops agents via: docker exec pkill -f 'opencode serve.*port <port>'

Port mapping:
- Main agent: 4096 (started by entrypoint)
- Sub-agents: 4001, 4002, 4003... (started by control plane)

No extra scripts needed - just docker exec + opencode's native API.

title: Agent spawner service inside container
description: Service that manages spawning additional opencode serve instances within a container. Allocates ports dynamically from a range (e.g., 41830-41899), tracks running agents, provides health checks. Could be a simple shell script or small Go/Rust binary.

title: Frontend: Agent display and filtering in session sidebar

   │ Documentation specialist        │
   │ [Start Chat]                    │
   ├─────────────────────────────────┤
   │ + Create New Agent              │
   └─────────────────────────────────┘

2. Agent status indicator:
   - Running (green dot) - opencode serve is active
   - Stopped (gray dot) - directory exists but not running
   - Starting (spinner) - being started

3. 'Start Chat' action:
   - If agent not running, call POST /session/{id}/agents to start it
   - Create new opencode session with that agent
   - Navigate to chat view

4. '+ Create New Agent' action:
   - Opens modal with:
     - Name input (becomes directory name)
     - Description textarea (becomes AGENTS.md content)
   - Creates directory via fileserver
   - Starts agent via control plane
   - Refreshes agent list

Data source:
  GET /session/{id}/agents returns:
  [
    { id: 'main', directory: '~/workspace', status: 'running', port: 4096 },
    { id: 'deep-research', directory: '~/workspace/deep-research', status: 'stopped', port: null },
    ...
  ]

title: Hot-reload agents when new directory is created
description:
When a new agent directory is created at runtime, start its opencode serve without container restart.

Options:

Option A: File watcher in entrypoint
- Use inotifywait to watch ~/workspace/ for new directories
- When new dir with AGENTS.md appears, spawn opencode serve
- Requires inotify-tools in container

Option B: API endpoint to start agent
- POST /agents/{name}/start
- Called by frontend after creating agent directory
- Simpler, explicit control

Option C: Periodic scan
- Every N seconds, check for new agent dirs
- Start any that aren't running
- Simple but has delay

Recommendation: Option B (explicit API)
- Frontend creates directory
- Frontend calls start endpoint
- Immediate feedback
- No background processes needed

Endpoint implementation:
POST /start-agent
{
  "directory": "/home/dev/workspace/doc-writer"
}

- Validate directory exists and has AGENTS.md
- Find next available port
- Start opencode serve in background
- Return port number

title: Control plane: Agent management API
description:
Add endpoints to the control plane for managing opencode agent instances within a container.

Endpoints:

POST /session/{id}/agents
  Start a new agent in the container
  Body: { "directory": "doc-writer" }  // relative to ~/workspace/
  Implementation:
    docker exec -d <container> bash -c 'cd ~/workspace/doc-writer && opencode serve --port 4001 --hostname 0.0.0.0'
  Returns: { "id": "doc-writer", "port": 4001, "status": "starting" }

GET /session/{id}/agents
  List all agents (running + available directories)
  Implementation:
    - Scan ~/workspace/*/ for directories with AGENTS.md or .git (via fileserver /tree or docker exec ls)
    - For each known agent port, check if opencode is responding (GET http://container:port/path)
  Returns: [{ "id": "main", "port": 4096, "status": "running", "directory": "..." }, ...]

DELETE /session/{id}/agents/{agent_id}
  Stop an agent
  Implementation:
    - Find process: docker exec <container> pgrep -f 'opencode serve.*port 4001'
    - Kill it: docker exec <container> pkill -f 'opencode serve.*port 4001'
  Returns: { "stopped": true }

GET /session/{id}/agents/{agent_id}
  Get agent status
  Implementation:
    - Try GET http://container:<port>/path
    - If responds, running; if not, stopped
  Returns: { "id": "doc-writer", "port": 4001, "status": "running" }

Port allocation:
  - main (workspace root) = 4096 (started by entrypoint)
  - sub-agents = 4001, 4002, 4003... (control plane tracks which ports are in use)

State tracking:
  - Control plane keeps in-memory map of session_id -> [(agent_id, port)]
  - On restart, rediscover by scanning ports 4001-4099 for responding opencode instances
  - Or just query opencode API on known ports

title: Container: Agent helper script for control plane
description:
Add a simple helper script in the container that the control plane can call via docker exec to manage agents.

Script: /usr/local/bin/agent-ctl

Usage:
  agent-ctl start <directory> [--port <port>]
    Start opencode serve in given directory
    Writes PID to /tmp/agents/<name>.pid
    Returns JSON: { "pid": 1234, "port": 4001 }

  agent-ctl stop <name>
    Stop agent by name (reads PID from pidfile)
    Returns JSON: { "stopped": true }

  agent-ctl list
    List running agents
    Returns JSON array of agents with pid, port, directory

  agent-ctl status <name>
    Check if agent is running
    Returns JSON: { "running": true, "pid": 1234, "port": 4001 }

Why a script instead of raw docker exec?
- Cleaner interface for control plane
- Handles PID tracking
- Can do port allocation logic
- Easier to test in isolation
- Single place for agent lifecycle logic

Implementation:
- Bash script (simple, no dependencies)
- PIDs stored in /tmp/agents/<name>.pid
- Ports stored in /tmp/agents/<name>.port
- Main agent (workspace root) always has id='main'

title: Backend: Track agent per opencode session
description:
Each opencode session needs to know which agent it belongs to.

Current state:
- opencode sessions are tracked internally by opencode
- We proxy to opencode via /session/{container_id}/code/*

New requirement:
- Frontend needs to know which agent a session belongs to
- Session list should show agent name
- Filter by agent

Options:

Option A: Store in opencode session metadata
- opencode may support custom metadata per session
- Check if we can pass/retrieve agent info

Option B: Derive from session's working directory
- Query opencode session info
- Parse working directory to extract agent name
- ~/workspace -> 'main'
- ~/workspace/deep-research -> 'deep-research'

Option C: Track mapping in control plane
- When creating session, store (opencode_session_id, agent_id) mapping
- Return agent info when listing sessions

Recommendation: Option B (derive from directory)
- No extra storage needed
- opencode already knows its working directory
- Just need to parse it when listing sessions

Implementation:
- When fetching session list from opencode, include 'path' or 'cwd' field
- Parse to extract agent name
- Include in session response to frontend

title: Backend: Create agent directory and AGENTS.md
description:
Endpoint to create a new agent directory with AGENTS.md file.

Endpoint:
POST /session/{id}/agents/create
Body: {
  "name": "doc-writer",
  "description": "Documentation and technical writing specialist"
}

Implementation:
1. Validate name (lowercase, alphanumeric, hyphens only)
2. Check directory doesn't already exist
3. Create directory: ~/workspace/{name}/
4. Create AGENTS.md with content:
   # {name}
   
   {description}
5. Optionally: git init the directory
6. Return: { "id": "doc-writer", "directory": "/home/dev/workspace/doc-writer" }

Can be implemented via:
- docker exec mkdir + echo (simple)
- fileserver PUT /mkdir + POST /file (cleaner, already have the APIs)

Recommendation: Use fileserver
- PUT /mkdir?path=doc-writer to create directory
- POST /file?path=doc-writer/AGENTS.md to write file
- Control plane orchestrates both calls

title: Agent directory structure convention
description: Define the convention for agent subdirectories: workspace/<agent_name>/AGENTS.md for agent-specific instructions. Document how agents discover their root, how they're named, and what files they expect. Example: workspace/document_agent/ with its own AGENTS.md that says 'You are a documentation specialist...'

title: Backend API: Register and track sub-agents
description: Extend the session model to track sub-agents. New endpoints: POST /sessions/{id}/agents (spawn sub-agent), GET /sessions/{id}/agents (list), DELETE /sessions/{id}/agents/{agent_id} (stop). Each sub-agent has: id, name, port, workdir, status, created_at.

title: Frontend: Multi-agent session view
description: Update session UI to show multiple agents. Tabbed interface or sidebar listing agents within a session. Each agent tab shows its own terminal/chat. Ability to spawn new agents from UI with name and optional AGENTS.md template.

title: Proxy routing for sub-agents
description: Update backend proxy to route to sub-agents. Routes like /session/{id}/agent/{agent_id}/code/* should proxy to the correct port. May need to query the agent registry or use a naming convention for container-internal routing.

title: Refactor container lifecycle: stop without remove
description:
Change session service to keep containers alive instead of removing them on stop.

Current behavior (wasteful):
- stop_session() -> stop container -> remove container
- Next start -> create brand new container (cold boot)

New behavior:
- stop_session() -> stop container (keep it)
- start/resume -> docker start (fast, warm caches)
- delete_session() -> stop + remove (explicit cleanup)

Changes needed in backend/src/session/service.rs:
1. stop_session(): Remove the remove_container() call
2. Add resume_session() that does docker start on stopped container
3. create_session(): Check if stopped container exists for user, restart it instead
4. Add cleanup job for containers stopped > N days (configurable)
5. upgrade_session(): Only case that needs remove (new image)

Benefits:
- Faster restarts (docker start vs create+start)
- Warm caches (opencode state, MCP servers initialized)
- Agent processes preserved
- Less container churn

title: Update entrypoint.sh to start multiple agents
description:
Modify container/entrypoint.sh to scan workspace for agent subdirectories and start an opencode serve instance for each.

Directory structure:
- ~/workspace/              -> Main agent (port 4096, default)
- ~/workspace/doc-writer/   -> Agent on port 4001
- ~/workspace/code-review/  -> Agent on port 4002
- etc.

Detection logic:
- Scan ~/workspace/*/ for directories containing AGENTS.md or .git
- Skip regular files and non-agent directories
- Main workspace always gets port 4096

Port mapping:
- Main agent: 4096 (already exposed)
- Sub-agents: 4001, 4002, 4003... (alphabetical order)

Implementation:
1. Start main agent in ~/workspace on port 4096 (current behavior)
2. Loop through ~/workspace/*/ directories
3. If dir has AGENTS.md or .git, start opencode serve on next port
4. Track PIDs for graceful shutdown
5. Log which agents started on which ports

Example output:
  Starting main agent on port 4096 (~/workspace)
  Starting agent 'doc-writer' on port 4001 (~/workspace/doc-writer)
  Starting agent 'code-review' on port 4002 (~/workspace/code-review)

title: Add agent discovery endpoint to fileserver or backend
description:
Agent discovery now goes through the control plane, not fileserver.

Control plane endpoint:
GET /session/{id}/agents
  Returns list of agents (running + available directories)

Response format:
{
  "agents": [
    {
      "id": "main",
      "name": "Main Workspace",
      "directory": "/home/dev/workspace",
      "port": 4096,
      "color": "#3B82F6",
      "status": "running",
      "hasAgentsMd": true,
      "hasGit": true
    },
    {
      "id": "doc-writer",
      "name": "doc-writer", 
      "directory": "/home/dev/workspace/doc-writer",
      "port": 4001,
      "color": "#10B981",
      "status": "running",
      "hasAgentsMd": true,
      "hasGit": true
    },
    {
      "id": "code-review",
      "name": "code-review",
      "directory": "/home/dev/workspace/code-review",
      "port": null,
      "color": "#F59E0B",
      "status": "stopped",
      "hasAgentsMd": true,
      "hasGit": false
    }
  ]
}

Implementation:
- Control plane calls 'docker exec agent-ctl list' to get running agents
- Also scans workspace subdirs (via fileserver or exec) for available but stopped agents
- Merges both lists
- Derives color from agent name hash (no storage needed)

title: Backend proxy routing for sub-agents
description:
Update backend to route requests to the correct agent port within a container.

Current routing:
  /session/{id}/code/* -> container:4096

New routing:
  /session/{id}/code/*                -> container:4096 (main agent)
  /session/{id}/agent/{name}/code/*   -> container:400X (sub-agent)

Port resolution:
  - 'main' or empty -> 4096
  - other names -> lookup port from agent list (4001 + index)

Implementation in backend/src/api/proxy.rs or similar:
1. Parse agent name from path
2. If agent specified, resolve port (4001 + alphabetical index of agent name)
3. Proxy to container_ip:resolved_port

Alternative: Query /agents endpoint to get name->port mapping
  - Cache for short duration (agents don't change often)
  - Refresh on 404 (new agent may have been created)

title: Live voice mode
description:
Add live voice interaction mode for hands-free agent communication.

## Features

### 1. Voice Input
- Browser microphone capture (MediaRecorder API)
- Stream audio to backend via WebSocket
- Speech-to-text processing (options):
  - OpenAI Whisper API
  - Local Whisper (in container)
  - Browser Web Speech API (fallback)

### 2. Voice Output  
- Text-to-speech for agent responses
- Options:
  - Browser SpeechSynthesis API (free, basic)
  - ElevenLabs API (quality, paid)
  - OpenAI TTS (quality, paid)
- Streaming TTS for faster response

### 3. Voice Mode UI
- Floating voice button (like Siri/Alexa)
- Visual feedback during:
  - Listening (pulsing indicator)
  - Processing (thinking animation)
  - Speaking (waveform)
- Push-to-talk or wake word activation
- Mute/unmute controls

### 4. Conversation Flow
- Voice input -> STT -> Agent -> TTS -> Voice output
- Interrupt handling (user speaks while agent responds)
- Conversation history synced with text chat

## Dependencies
- workspace-45p (WebSocket support)

## Configuration
- Voice provider selection in settings
- Voice/language preferences
- Wake word customization (future)

Frontend UI Architecture - Professional & Extensible App System

Each sidebar entry represents a self-contained app with its own routes, components, and state. No emojis, no ugly icons - clean typography, subtle visual hierarchy, and enterprise-grade polish. Key goals: (1) Extensible app registry pattern for adding new sidebar apps, (2) Professional dark-first design system with semantic colors, (3) Responsive split-pane layouts with persistence, (4) Clean monospace typography with visual hierarchy through weight/spacing not icons.

title: Design System - Professional Color Palette & Typography

Define CSS custom properties for: (1) Base colors - dark backgrounds (210 10% 8-15%), muted foregrounds, accent teal/cyan, (2) Semantic colors - primary, secondary, muted, destructive with foreground pairs, (3) Sidebar-specific tokens, (4) Typography - JetBrains Mono or similar monospace, uppercase tracking for labels, normal for content, (5) Spacing scale (4px base), (6) Border radius (subtle 0.25-0.5rem). title: Voice mode continues playing after stop button clicked
